india-medtech-sector-growth-2030-exports:
Summary:

By 2030, the medical technology (medtech) sector in India is predicted to grow to a US$35 billion (₹3.33 lakh crore) business due to increased healthcare demand, exports, and innovation. Medical device exports are expected to increase by more than 20% a year and reach US$8 billion by 2030, according to a Bain & Company analysis. With robust expansion, India's healthcare sector is also anticipated to surpass US$320 billion. India, which exports medical equipment to more than 125 nations, is known for creating cutting-edge and reasonably priced healthcare products. Stronger clinical trials, manufacturing, research, and regulatory frameworks will all contribute to future growth. This growth will increase employment, enhance access to healthcare, lower imports, and solidify India's standing as a major worldwide center for medical technology.

Source: IBEF

india-asean-trade-agreement-review-aitig:
Summary:

From July 6 -10, 2026, India held the 13th Joint Committee meeting to review the ASEAN-India Trade in Goods Agreement (AITIGA) in New Delhi. Modernising the trade agreement to enhance market access, streamline trade processes, and fortify economic cooperation with ASEAN was the main goal of the summit. Participants in the talks included representatives from each of the ten ASEAN members. About 11% of India's international commerce is with ASEAN, with bilateral trade amounting to US$128 billion in 2025–2026. Trade facilitation, origin regulations, and customs processes were also reviewed. In addition to supporting India's Act East Policy and sustainable regional economy, the revised agreement is anticipated to increase trade, draw in additional investment, fortify regional supply chains, and open up new commercial possibilities.

Source: IBEF

india-energy-storage-growth-bess-demand-:
Summary:

The demand for energy storage systems (ESS) is estimated to rise from 1 GWh to 888 GWh by 2035 - 2036, indicating the significant growth of India's energy storage industry. Battery energy storage systems (BESS) are crucial for enhancing grid resilience and promoting renewable energy, according to the India BESS Market Review. By year's end, installed BESS capacity is predicted to surpass 10 GWh, having increased from 0.78 GWh in December 2025 to 8.70 GWh in the first half of 2026. India's ability to produce batteries is also growing dramatically. Maintaining energy storage investments will boost local manufacturing, increase energy security, facilitate the integration of renewable energy sources, improve grid flexibility, and assist India in achieving its long-term net-zero and sustainable development objectives.

Source: IBEF

india-australia-partnership-trade-techno:
Summary:

In an effort to fortify the India-Australia Comprehensive Strategic Partnership, Prime Minister Narendra Modi will go to Australia on July 8 -10, 2026. The goal of the visit is to increase commerce, investment, technology, key minerals, education, and interpersonal relationships. Thanks to continuing talks on the Comprehensive Economic Cooperation Agreement (CECA) and the India-Australia Economic Cooperation and commerce Agreement (IndAus ECTA), bilateral commerce has reached US$33.93 billion. Australia sells coal, natural gas, and copper ores, whereas India exports textiles, jewellery, medicines, and petroleum products. The alliance is anticipated to increase investment, fortify supply chains, enhance market access, foster cooperation in developing industries, and advance sustainable economic growth and regional connectivity throughout the Indo-Pacific.

Source: IBEF

google-cloud-gemini-ai-india-data-sovere:
Summary:

Google Cloud has installed its most recent Gemini AI models on Indian infrastructure, enabling businesses to handle all of their data and AI tasks domestically. This lowers latency, promotes data sovereignty, fortifies India's digital infrastructure, and assists companies in adhering to regional laws. Gemini Flash and Gemini Enterprise's local hosting will help industries including banking, healthcare, telecommunications, and government. With a US$15 billion investment, Google Cloud also intends to increase data center infrastructure and AI engineering skills in India. It is anticipated that these activities would boost cloud infrastructure, accelerate the use of AI, generate high-value employment, encourage digital transformation, and establish India as a major worldwide center for AI and cutting-edge technological innovation.

tata-motors-growth-plan-fy31-electric-ve:
Summary:

By FY31, Tata Motors intends to develop a US$100 billion automotive industry with significant investments in its commercial, passenger, and Jaguar Land Rover (JLR) sectors. The business will make significant investments to boost electric mobility, diversify its product line, and increase production. Additionally, it wants to keep a 40–45% market share for electric passenger cars in India. Efficiency, creativity, and strategic focus are anticipated to increase with the proposed demerger of its passenger and commercial vehicle operations. These investments will improve local manufacturing, boost global competitiveness, promote next-generation mobility solutions, and solidify India's standing as a major global center for the automotive and electric vehicle industries.

india-ev-sales-growth-june-2026-electric:
Summary:

According to the Federation of Automobile Dealers Associations (FADA), retail sales of electric vehicles (EVs) in India reached 3,06,220 units in June 2026, a 63% year-over-year growth, marking a historic milestone. Additionally, EV penetration for the first time exceeded 12%, indicating rising customer acceptability. The rise was led by electric two-wheelers, and the monthly sales of passenger and commercial EVs reached all-time highs. This accomplishment was made possible by favourable market conditions, increased product availability, and expanding charging infrastructure. Growing EV use in both personal and commercial transportation indicates growing consumer confidence and industry preparedness, according to FADA. India's position as one of the fastestgrowing electric mobility markets in the world is being strengthened by ongoing investments in infrastructure and innovation, which are also promoting sustainability and clean transportation objectives.

Source: IBEF

iim-bangalore-indonesia-campus-global-ed:
Summary:

This article discusses Prime Minister Narendra Modi's announcement during his visit to Indonesia that IIM Bangalore will develop its first overseas campus there. The goal of the new campus in Malang's Singhasari Special Economic Zone is to provide students from Indonesia and throughout Southeast Asia a top-notch management education. By increasing the international visibility of top Indian universities, it advances the objectives of India's National Education Policy 2020. Programs in artificial intelligence, digital transformation, global supply chains, climate and sustainability, and healthcare management will be available on campus. Prior to starting degree programs, executive education will be the first step. Additionally, regional academic interchange and educational collaboration between Indonesia and India will be strengthened.

Source: IBEF

india-auto-components-growth-fy27-export:
Summary:

The Automotive Component Manufacturers Association (ACMA) projects that despite worldwide geopolitical risks, India's auto components sector would expand by 8–10% in FY27 thanks to robust local demand and growing exports. With a sales of ₹7.60 lakh crore (US$85.9 billion), the industry had a 12.7% increase over the previous fiscal year. Export orders, replacement markets, and original equipment manufacturers' (OEMs') need all continued to fuel growth. According to ACMA, investments in manufacturing excellence, technology advancements, and localisation are boosting global competitiveness. While long-term industrial development will be supported by innovation and advantageous legislation, growing localisation and improved global supply chain integration are anticipated to minimise import dependency, even while imports of electronics and EV components remain high.

Source: IBEF

india-semicon-2-policy-semiconductor-eco:
Summary:

India is getting ready to introduce Semicon 2.0, a more comprehensive semiconductor strategy that offers government assistance throughout the whole semiconductor value chain. Chip design companies, assembly, testing, marking, and packaging (ATMP) units, equipment makers, material suppliers, and research universities will all receive incentives from the project. It seeks to increase supply chain resilience, lessen reliance on imports, and fortify the domestic semiconductor ecosystem. Additionally, the policy emphasises worker training, research and development, and helping semiconductor entrepreneurs establish their own skills. Semicon 2.0 aims to attract investments, foster innovation, generate skilled employment, and establish India as an internationally competitive hub for advanced electronics and semiconductor production by fostering collaboration between industry, academia, and technology partners.

Source: IBEF

jharkhand-mango-export-dubai-women-farme:
Summary:

With the sale of two metric tonnes of Amrapali mangoes to Dubai from Gumla and Deoghar in Jharkhand, India bolstered its agricultural export ecosystem. Three women-led Farmer Producer Companies (FPCs) with support from the Jharkhand State Livelihood Promotion Society (JSLPS) provided the consignment, which was facilitated by the Agricultural and Processed Food Products Export Development Authority (APEDA). With assistance from MGNREGA, tribal women established the orchards under the Birsa Harit Gram Yojana. When compared to local market pricing, the program raised farmers' revenues by about 180%. Additionally, APEDA promoted women-led entrepreneurship, rural livelihoods, and Jharkhand's horticulture export potential through value-added agricultural exports while enhancing export readiness through training on export standards and post-harvest procedures.

Source: IBEF

tata-power-growth-plan-2030-renewable-en:
Summary:

Tata Power has announced a long-term plan to reach ₹10,000 crore in profit and ₹1 lakh crore in sales by 2030. To spur expansion, the corporation intends to diversify into manufacturing, renewable energy, transmission, distribution, and power generation. To increase domestic renewable energy generation, it would build a new 10 GW solar equipment manufacturing facility in Odisha as part of its clean energy agenda. As regulatory changes allow the private sector to participate, Tata Power also intends to investigate potential in nuclear power, particularly small modular reactors (SMRs). It also intends to increase the size of its power distribution network, make investments in cutting-edge technology and renewable energy, and assist India's energy transformation by building power infrastructure that is secure, sustainable, and prepared for the future.

Source: IBEF

india-leather-footwear-exports-growth-ft:
Summary:

India's leather and footwear sector should aim for exports of Rs. 1.40 lakh crore (US$15 billion) in five to six years by utilising Free Trade Agreements (FTAs), entering new markets, and enhancing quality, branding, design, and sustainability, according to Union Minister of Commerce and Industry Piyush Goyal. He stated that the industry may increase exports from the present US$4–4.5 billion while speaking at the Council for Leather Exports National Export Excellence Awards 2024– 2025. He emphasised diversity, cutting-edge technology, improved packaging, and environmental compliance while pointing out that free trade agreements (FTAs) give access to 38 industrialised nations. Goyal stated that with sustained government backing and modernisation efforts, the industry, which currently employs over 40 lakh people, could generate one crore employment.

Source: IBEF

india-indonesia-relations-trade-investme:
Summary:

On July 6–8, 2026, Prime Minister Narendra Modi will make his first bilateral visit to Indonesia after the two nations formed a Comprehensive Strategic Partnership in 2018. Strengthening collaboration in commerce, investment, connectivity, and critical areas is the goal of the visit. In the ASEAN area, Indonesia is India's second-largest commercial partner. In 2024–2025, bilateral trade reached Rs. 2,39,275 crore (US$28.15 billion). India purchased US$22.78 billion from Indonesia and exported US$5.38 billion worth of products. Indian investments in Indonesia amounted US$1.56 billion over 7,292 projects between 2000 and 2024. Growing economic and interpersonal links are further reinforced by increasing air connectivity and tourism.

Source: IBEF

india-seafood-exports-efta-tepa-market-a:
Summary:

In order to explore prospects for India's seafood industry under the India-European Free Trade Association (EFTA) Trade and Economic Partnership Agreement (TEPA), the Department of Commerce hosted a Chintan Shivir at the Chennai Trade Center on July 3, 2026, during Seafood Expo Bharat 2026. The purpose of the event was to increase fish exports to Norway, Switzerland, Iceland, and Liechtenstein. With access to economies with a combined GDP of US$1.79 trillion, TEPA, India's first active trade deal with a European economic union, seeks to draw in US$100 billion in investments. The agreement increases export competitiveness by providing tariff discounts. In order to boost seafood exports, government organisations also advised exporters on market access, quality standards, investment possibilities, and regulatory compliance.m.

indian-railways-new-trains-connectivity-:
Summary:

Through video conference, Union Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw launched the Tanakpur-Pilibhit service to Shahjahanpur and the Nanded-Mumbai Express and Tanakpur-Nanded Express. In addition to giving the Sikh community direct access to Hazur Sahib and bolstering transit connections in Maharashtra, the new services will increase connectivity throughout Uttar Pradesh, Uttarakhand, and Maharashtra. Additionally, the Tanakpur-Agra service has been regularised. The Ministry emphasised significant advancements in railway infrastructure, including as 99.60% electrification and 37,000 km of new lines. While freight corridors are growing, the MumbaiAhmedabad Bullet Train project is moving forward. Additionally, Indian Railways promised more special trains for key festivals and regional travel, as well as the deployment of 4,000 new regular coaches.

indiahandmade-digital-marketplace-artisa:
Summary:

Through direct connections with buyers across the country, Indiahandmade, a digital marketplace created by the Digital India Corporation under the Ministry of Textiles, is increasing market access for India's handloom and handicraft craftsmen. The portal, which was introduced in 2023, promotes traditional crafts and rural lives while showcasing GI-tagged and One District One Product (ODOP) goods. Through streamlined onboarding, direct sales, and less reliance on middlemen, it helps over 64.66 lakh craftspeople, many of whom are women. Using an Enrolment ID, sellers who are not registered for GST can also take part in intra-state sales. Indiahandmade hopes to enrol over 60 lakh artisans and bolster India's worldwide craft footprint by providing safe payments, free delivery, and a variety of handcrafted goods.

ashok-leyland-vehicle-scrappage-rosmerta:
Summary:

Commercial vehicle manufacturer Ashok Leyland has partnered with Rosmerta Recycling to strengthen India’s organised vehicle scrappage ecosystem and encourage the replacement of ageing commercial vehicles with cleaner, fuel-efficient models. Through its dealer network, customers will receive seamless access to authorised Registered Vehicle Scrapping Facilities (RVSFs), including support for deregistration and documentation. The initiative aligns with the Government’s Vehicle Scrappage Policy and the Naya Safar Scheme to reduce transport emissions. Rosmerta Recycling will ensure safe and environmentally responsible vehicle dismantling while supporting the circular economy. Customers will benefit from competitive scrap value, OEM discounts and applicable tax waivers, promoting sustainable mobility, improved road safety and cleaner commercial transportation across India.

mumbai-ahmedabad-bullet-train-india-high:
Summary:

India's first bullet train project, the 508-kilometer Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail (MAHSR) line, is almost finished and will serve as the basis for a uniform high-speed rail network. The train, which can reach up to 350 kmph, will take around one hour and fifty-eight minutes to get from Mumbai to Ahmedabad. In August 2027, the first operational segment between Surat and Vapi is anticipated to open. The project encourages indigenous manufacturing under the Make in India and Aatmanirbhar Bharat agendas using locally produced high-speed train sets. Building on this experience, India has proposed seven new high-speed corridors encompassing over 4,000 km, intending to boost connectivity, cut travel time, strengthen industry, and support long-term economic growth.

india-russia-investment-target-50-billio:
Summary:

India and Russia have set a target of achieving Rs. 4,41,850 crore (US$ 50 billion) in mutual investments by 2030 to strengthen their economic partnership. The target was discussed at the India-Russia Joint Working Group meeting in Moscow, led by the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) and Russia's Ministry of Economic Development. Both countries agreed to promote private sector participation and joint investments in advanced manufacturing, green hydrogen, energy storage, critical minerals, mining and emerging technologies. India highlighted its economic reforms, investment opportunities and skilled workforce to attract Russian investors. The initiative aims to expand industrial cooperation, strengthen supply chains, boost technology collaboration and support long-term economic growth in both countries.

india-real-estate-investment-growth-q2-2:
Summary:

India's real estate sector recorded a 70% year-on-year rise in institutional investments to Rs. 27,045.40 crore (US$ 2.9 billion) during the second quarter of 2026, driven by strong domestic and foreign investor participation. According to a Colliers report, office spaces remained the top investment destination, followed by mixed-use developments, data centres and hospitality assets. Total investments in the first half of 2026 reached Rs. 41,566.5 crore (US$ 4.5 billion), the highest first-half inflow in six years. Chennai and Bengaluru were the leading investment destinations, supported by strong office demand. Rising capital inflows, diversified investments and India's improved economic outlook continue to strengthen investor confidence in the country's long-term real estate growth.

india-green-hydrogen-global-supplier-cle:
Summary:

India is emerging as a reliable global supplier of green fuels as its green hydrogen sector achieves commercial maturity. According to Renewable Energy Secretary Santosh Kumar Sarangi, India is well positioned to play a key role in global green hydrogen and green ammonia value chains due to its strong renewable energy base and expanding production capacity. At the India-Japan Energy Partnership Forum, officials highlighted growing international partnerships with Indian companies. ACME Group has signed long-term agreements to supply green ammonia and green methanol to Japan. These collaborations are expected to strengthen global clean energy supply chains, support industrial decarbonisation, attract investments and technology, and reinforce India's position in the global renewable energy market.

dpiit-startup-innovation-challenge-fundi:
Summary:

The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) is strengthening India's startup ecosystem through the FICCI Mercedes-Benz Bharat Innovation & Business Ideas Challenge Programme. The initiative supports innovative startups in manufacturing, sustainability, education, decarbonisation and automotive/EV sectors. Seven selected startups will receive Rs. 30 lakh each along with structured mentoring to accelerate growth and improve investment readiness. They will also receive guidance on legal compliance, taxation, fundraising, valuation, cybersecurity, data privacy and intellectual property protection. Demo Days and the Sounding Board Programme will connect startups with investors, mentors and industry leaders. The initiative aims to promote innovation, entrepreneurship, technology-led growth and globally competitive businesses while supporting India's long-term economic development.

gujarat-samarth-semiconductor-hub-iit-ga:
Summary:

Gujarat is strengthening India's semiconductor ecosystem by establishing the Silicon and Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Research and Training Hub (SAMARTH) at IIT Gandhinagar with an investment of Rs. 190 crore (US$ 19.96 million). Jointly funded by MeitY, the Gujarat Government and IIT Gandhinagar, the hub will support research, innovation and workforce development in semiconductor manufacturing. It will provide advanced infrastructure and practical training for students, faculty, technicians and industry professionals, creating a pipeline of "fabready" engineers. SAMARTH will promote collaboration between academia, industry and government while enhancing technical expertise. The initiative supports India's goal of semiconductor self-reliance, strengthens domestic technological capabilities and boosts the longterm growth of the electronics manufacturing ecosystem.

india-private-credit-market-growth-fy30-:
Summary:

According to Moody's Ratings, the assets under management in India's private credit market are likely to double from Rs. 2.11 lakh crore (US$25 billion) in 2025 to Rs. 4.72 lakh crore (US$50 billion) by FY30. The need for alternative finance sources, infrastructure projects, and growing business financing requirements are the main drivers of the market's expansion. Due to stable economic conditions and a wide range of investors, private lending is becoming a popular financing alternative. About 40% of total assets are made up of real estate, with infrastructure and promoter finance coming in second. Larger transactions are anticipated as a result of increased fund availability, bolstering India's alternative financing ecosystem and promoting long-term infrastructure development and economic growth.

india-toy-industry-export-growth-fta-uk-:
Summary:

Free trade agreements (FTAs) with the UK and the EU are anticipated to provide India's toy industry with a significant export boost. By granting Indian toys zero-duty access, these accords will increase their competitiveness in important export countries, which account for around 16% of India's toy exports. The India-UK FTA takes effect on July 15, 2026, while the India-EU pact is due next year. Government-friendly policies are also encouraging investments. Playgro Toys India has invested Rs. 82 crore (US$ 8.61 million) in a manufacturing facility at Ujjain, creating 500 employment, while Sunlord Group is establishing a unit in Bihar. To enhance international export potential, industry professionals emphasise enhancing quality, testing infrastructure, and sustainability.

india-japan-summit-ai-defence-clean-ener:
Summary:

At the 16th India-Japan Annual Summit, Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi reached agreements on military, artificial intelligence (AI), economic security, clean energy, and emerging technologies, strengthening the India-Japan Special Strategic and Global Partnership. The nations adopted a Joint Statement on AI and inked their first defence co-development project for a naval radio antenna. More than Rs. 94,380 crore (US$10 billion) in Japanese investment is anticipated to reach India through more than 100 commercial agreements. Along with initiatives in biogas, sustainable transportation, semiconductors, and advanced technologies to foster innovation, resilient supply chains, and long-term strategic cooperation, both countries also adopted statements on economic security and energy resilience.

india-ayush-exports-growth-fta-innovatio:
Summary:

To bolster India's position as a global leader in the AYUSH industry, the Department of Commerce, the Ministry of AYUSH, and the AYUSH Export Promotion Council (AYUSHEXCIL) sponsored a Government-Industry Brainstorming Session in New Delhi. The event focused on innovation, quality, exports, and international collaboration, bringing together government officials, exporters, MSMEs, startups, researchers, and industry representatives. Opportunities under free trade agreements (FTAs), export facilitation, worldwide branding, quality standards, WHO-GMP compliance, and scientific validation were all discussed. The Government reaffirmed its commitment to promote AYUSH as a major export sector through programs such as Ayush Mark and Ayurveda Aahar, while boosting market access, competitiveness, innovation, and worldwide recognition.

ibm-quantum-computer-india-amaravati-qua:
Summary:

One of India's first two IBM quantum computers will be located in Amaravati, Andhra Pradesh, and is expected to be commissioned in September 2026, according to an announcement made by International Business Machines (IBM). The system will give researchers, startups, universities, and corporations with enhanced quantum computing capabilities for applications in medicines, materials science, finance, logistics, cybersecurity, and Artificial Intelligence (AI). The project backs Andhra Pradesh's Quantum Valley effort, which uses industry, academia, and government cooperation to build India's first integrated quantum ecosystem. Research initiatives and a specialised Quantum Valley Tech Park will foster creativity, talent development, and startup expansion. The effort is designed to increase India's quantum capabilities, expedite deep-tech research, and enhance global competitiveness.

tata-technologies-tenneco-deal-engineeri:
Summary:

Through a strategic engineering and digital transformation deal worth more than Rs. 944 crore (US$100 million) for the next five years, Tata Technologies and Tenneco LLC have deepened their cooperation. With work based in Tata Technologies' Global Engineering Center in Pune, the partnership will improve engineering, digital technologies, and business transformation throughout Tenneco's international operations. To boost product innovation and boost operational effectiveness, the company will offer embedded software, AI-enabled digital enterprise services, and engineering research and development. Building on their 2021 cooperation, the deal will enhance India's role in global engineering services, assist automotive technology innovation, strengthen the engineering research environment, and promote digital transformation and advanced manufacturing capabilities.

upi-growth-june-2026-digital-payments-in:
Summary:

In June 2026, India's Unified Payments Interface (UPI) saw robust growth, with transaction values rising 20% to over Rs. 28 lakh crore and transaction volumes up 23% year over year to over 22 billion. With an average of 757 million transactions processed every day, UPI's growing significance in India's digital payments ecosystem is evident. The ongoing increase is a result of increased customer confidence in cashless transactions, broader merchant acceptance, and more financial inclusion. Currently, UPI is available in over eight nations, including the United Arab Emirates, Singapore, France, Mauritius, Sri Lanka, and Greece. Cross-border payments and India's position as a global leader in digital financial infrastructure and innovation are further strengthened by Eurobank's new UPI-based remittance service from Greece to India.

digital-india-initiative-growth-innovati:
Summary:

As he celebrated the 11th anniversary of the Digital India initiative, Prime Minister Narendra Modi referred to it as the cornerstone of an advanced and independent India. Since its July 1, 2015 launch, the program has enhanced governance by increasing the accessibility, efficiency, and transparency of public services. Digital payments, Direct Benefit Transfers (DBT), and digital platforms for public services, healthcare, education, and agriculture have improved day-to-day living. By assisting businesses and startups, particularly in smaller cities and villages, Digital India has also fostered innovation. The initiative, which is based on nine fundamental pillars, has improved India's digital economy. Digital India continues to propel innovation, economic growth, and inclusive development with UPI at the forefront of global real-time payments and developments in AI, semiconductors, and quantum computing.

india-greece-trade-growth-fintech-infras:
Summary:

In order to improve trade, investment, digital payments, financial services, infrastructure, and emerging technologies, Union Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal met with senior Greek ministers. Fintech, industrial cooperation, aviation, maritime connectivity, sustainable mobility, and robust supply chains were all discussed, which reflects the two nations' expanding strategic alliance. Bilateral commerce between Greece and India rose from US$689.33 million in FY21 to US$1.68 billion in FY25, indicating growing business connections. By 2030, both countries hope to double bilateral trade to about US$4 billion through more investment, more robust corporate alliances, and diversified commerce in manufacturing, technology, pharmaceuticals, and renewable energy. It is anticipated that the collaboration will enhance supply chains, increase innovation, and increase India's market share in Europe.

india-gst-collections-june-2026-growth-t:
Summary:

As a result of increased economic activity and better tax compliance, India's gross Goods and Services Tax (GST) receipts increased 13.9% year over year to Rs. 1.95 lakh crore in June 2026. After refunds, net GST collections rose 11.2% to Rs. 1.62 lakh crore. While domestic GST collections increased by 6.5%, GST revenue from imports increased by 34.6%. Business liquidity was supported by a 29.1% increase in refunds. Gross GST collections from April to June of FY27 were Rs. 6.32 lakh crore, an increase of 8.4% over the previous year. Uttar Pradesh had significant growth, but Maharashtra had the highest domestic GST collections. The steady increase demonstrates improved compliance, a more robust tax system, and growing formal economic activity throughout India.

she-leaps-women-entrepreneurship-lakhpat:
Summary:

The Government has launched the Self-Help Entrepreneur-Livelihoods and Enterprise Application for Prosperity and Sustainability (SHE-LEAPS), a digital platform to strengthen women-led rural enterprises and support the Lakhpati Didi initiative. Developed by the Digital India Corporation under the LokOS platform, it enables enterprise creation, livelihood management and performance tracking for women in Self-Help Groups (SHGs). Implemented across 34 States and Union Territories, the platform improves governance, financial inclusion and data-driven decision-making. The Government has increased the Lakhpati Didi target from three crore to six crore women and plans to invest Rs. 10 lakh crore over five years. SHE-LEAPS aims to boost income generation, sustainable entrepreneurship and inclusive rural development across India.

ai-drug-discovery-mankind-pharma-denovo-:
Summary:

In an effort to boost pharmaceutical research and development in India, Mankind Pharma and Denovo Sciences have joined to build an AI-driven drug discovery initiative. The partnership accelerates early-stage drug discovery, enhances lead candidate selection, and shortens development timeframes by fusing Denovo Sciences' AI technology with Mankind Pharma's research experience. AI-generated molecular analysis will be supplemented by ongoing scientific validation using a human-in-the-loop technique to improve research efficiency and accuracy. It is anticipated that the program will increase trial success rates, cut down on time spent on less promising compounds, and lessen development expenses. Additionally, it draws attention to India's expanding use of AI in the life sciences, which promotes innovation, improved healthcare outcomes, and leadership in pharmaceutical research worldwide.

gst-india-tax-reform-unified-system-econ:
Summary:

The Goods and Services Tax (GST), which replaced several Central and State levies with a single framework, has completely changed India's indirect taxation structure since its implementation in 2017. Under the motto "One Nation, One Tax," it has decreased tax cascading, increased transparency, and established a single national market. Digital solutions for e-invoicing, tax payments, registration, and return filing have improved business operations and streamlined compliance. By rationalising tax rates, expanding exemptions, and streamlining procedures, the 2025 Next-Generation GST amendments substantially enhanced the system and benefited both consumers and businesses, particularly MSMEs. Cooperative federalism has been reinforced by GST, which has also increased tax efficiency, stimulated investment, and fostered inclusive, sustainable, and technology-driven economic growth throughout India.

india-growth-defence-innovation-manufact:
Summary:

According to Defence Minister Rajnath Singh, economic progress, scientific innovation, and national security are what propel India's aspirations to become a developed nation. He emphasised that the effort has developed into a national movement supporting the idea of Viksit Bharat while speaking at the Vibrant Gujarat Regional Conference. Through industry, MSMEs, tribal goods, heavy industries, and defence manufacture, Gujarat continues to make a substantial contribution. He underlined that the defence industry fosters employment, economic expansion, and technological innovation while bolstering national security. Manufacturing and domestic supply chains are being strengthened via defence corridors. According to Singh, increased independence, more robust industries, and consistent investment in manufacturing, research, and defence will boost India's competitiveness and quicken its development.

india-japan-strategic-partnership-trade-:
Summary:

Based on centuries of diplomatic, cultural, and spiritual connections, India and Japan have a Special Strategic and Global Partnership. Since their founding in 1952, diplomatic ties have developed through a number of alliances, culminating in the Special Strategic and Global Partnership in 2014. These days, collaboration encompasses trade, investment, infrastructure, research and technology, digital innovation, healthcare, sustainable energy, climate action, defence, and interpersonal interactions. Strengthening regional cooperation, India's Act East Policy, SAGAR, and Indo-Pacific Oceans Initiative complement Japan's Free and Open Indo-Pacific agenda. In order to strengthen their long-term strategic alliance, both countries signed important agreements at the 15th Annual Summit in 2025 and pledged to support Japanese private investment of Rs. 5.8 lakh crore.

upi-global-expansion-eurobank-greece-ind:
Summary:

With the introduction of a UPI-based remittance service by Eurobank for money transfers from Greece to India, India's digital payments ecosystem has grown internationally. The service, which was created in collaboration with NPCI International Payments Limited, makes cross-border UPI transactions quick, safe, and affordable. The program boosts UPI's expanding global reach, lowers remittance costs, and speeds up transactions. During his visit to Greece, Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal launched the service, calling it a significant step toward India's digital payment system's international expansion. In order to promote investment, manufacturing, infrastructure development, innovation, and sustainable economic growth, he also met with Fokion Karavias, CEO of Eurobank, to explore strengthening India-Greece economic collaboration.

tata-communications-subsea-cable-india-s:
Summary:

Tata Communications plans to invest Rs. 1,442.33 crore to extend its undersea cable infrastructure in order to improve digital connectivity between Singapore and India. For a total of 98 Tbps on the India-Singapore corridor, the investment consists of integrating the MIST Cable System, which has a 20 Tbps capacity, and taking part in Project CS, which adds 78 Tbps. The extension intends to provide high-speed, reliable, and low-latency connectivity to meet the increasing demand for digital services, cloud computing, and artificial intelligence. While Project CS is slated to be finished by Q3 FY31, the MIST Cable System is anticipated to be operational by Q4 FY27. India's position in Asia's digital ecosystem would be strengthened, network resilience will be improved, and regional digital transformation will be supported.

india-semiconductor-mission-2-chip-manuf:
Summary:

With the proposed India Semiconductor Mission (ISM) 2.0, which would provide Rs. 1.25 lakh crore and is awaiting Cabinet clearance, India has advanced its semiconductor goals. By investing in chip design, production, equipment, materials, indigenous intellectual property, and robust supply chains, the mission seeks to fortify the semiconductor ecosystem, building on ISM 1.0. To increase India's competitiveness internationally, it also prioritises developing talent and luring business partners. Twelve manufacturing projects totalling Rs. 1.64 lakh crore, comprising fabrication and packaging units, were sanctioned by the government under the first phase. Furthermore, 24 design initiatives, 105 businesses utilising cutting-edge chip design tools, and 23 tapeouts show notable advancements in the development of a robust domestic semiconductor manufacturing ecosystem in India.

india-electric-passenger-vehicle-growth-:
Summary:

The market for electric passenger vehicles (EPVs) in India is anticipated to surpass three lakh units sold annually in 2026 due to the introduction of new vehicles, enhanced infrastructure for charging, and growing customer acceptability. With registrations already approaching 1.5 lakh in the first half of 2026, sales are expected to increase from just under two lakh units in 2025. The festive season and subsequent launches are anticipated to drive demand further. According to Crisil, the number of available EPV models has risen to about 20 and is predicted to exceed 35 next year, particularly in the more reasonably priced category. Longer driving ranges, increased charging networks, and improved battery technology are boosting consumer trust and assisting India's shift to sustainable electric mobility.

india-seychelles-relations-mous-upi-heal:
Summary:

Through agreements in digital payments, healthcare, space, maritime cooperation, agriculture, and infrastructure, Prime Minister Narendra Modi's State Visit to the Seychelles improved bilateral relations. India and Seychelles signed Memorandums of Understanding to implement UPI, provide reasonably priced medications through the Jan Aushadhi Scheme, encourage peaceful space collaboration, and improve diplomatic, agricultural, and seafarer training. A Fast Patrol Vessel, utility cars, ambulances, boats, rice, cement and a Line of Credit for infrastructure projects were among the development aid that India also announced. Initiatives commemorating 50 years of diplomatic ties as well as cutting-edge defence and education projects were started by both countries. In order to strengthen collaboration in sustainable development, regional connectivity, and long-term economic partnership, Seychelles also joined the Coalition for Disaster Resilient Infrastructure.

india-food-exports-summer-fancy-food-sho:
Summary:

By opening its largest-ever India Pavilion at the Summer Fancy Food Show 2026 in New York, India enhanced its worldwide food export position. The pavilion, which is organised by APEDA and ITPO, has more than 70 vendors exhibiting premium mangoes, frozen meals, snacks, organic products, ready-to-eat foods, beverages, basmati rice, spices, and other agricultural products with added value. The event emphasises India's expanding importance in international food exports and promotes Indian brands among consumers worldwide. Over the next ten years, there will be substantial market potential due to the growing demand for authentic Indian cuisine in North America. The pavilion aids exporters in gaining access to new markets, forming enduring alliances, and boosting India's agricultural and processed food industries' ability to compete internationally.

india-dairy-sector-growth-fy27-demand-va:
Summary:

According to Crisil Ratings, the organised dairy sector in India is anticipated to grow more strongly in FY27, with revenue surpassing the projected 11% rise in FY26. Eight to ten percent volume expansion, increased milk prices, and consistent demand for goods like milk, butter, and ghee will all contribute to growth. The industry is anticipated to grow as customer preferences for valueadded dairy products - such as those that are high in protein and probiotics - increase. Companies are likely to sustain profitability through modest pricing adjustments, even though raw milk output may decline due to lower calf yields and higher fodder costs. India's organised dairy sector will be strengthened and long-term growth supported by sustained capital investment, solid financial conditions, and increased production capacity.

Summary:

Through the second National Seafood Skill Olympiad, which MPEDA is hosting during Seafood Expo Bharat 2026, India is bolstering its seafood processing industry. The program encourages increased value addition in seafood exports while fostering innovation, quality enhancement, and skill development. Over 2,500 professionals received training from MPEDA through 50 speciality programs prior to the event, and the best 10 finalists competed nationally for awards and cash prizes. Over 325 stalls will be present at Seafood Expo Bharat 2026, which is expected to draw about 5,000 industry participants. With the help of 670 processing facilities, India's seafood exports hit a record Rs. 74,267 crore in 2025–2026. Through sustained investment and innovation, MPEDA hopes to raise value-added marine products to 25% of all exports by 2030.

india-wealth-management-growth-aum-2030-:
Summary:

With assets under management expected to increase from Rs. 84.81 lakh crore in 2024 to Rs. 160.19 lakh crore by 2030, the asset and wealth management sector in India is expected to nearly treble. Between 2024 and 2030, it is predicted to increase at a CAGR of 11.6%, surpassing AsiaPacific's 6.8% growth. Institutional capital, retail involvement, regulatory changes, digital infrastructure, and GIFT City's rise to prominence as a global financial center are the main drivers of growth. These elements direct household savings into long-term investments and strengthen capital markets. India's financialisation is bolstered by the country's 80% bank account penetration rate and growing UPI digital payments. With increasing demat accounts, SIP inflows, and institutional funds from EPFO and insurance, UPI processes Rs. 235.58 lakh crore yearly.

Through trade, investment, connectivity, renewable energy, and collaboration on the blue economy, India and the Seychelles are fortifying their economic ties. Bilateral trade increased by about 15% from the previous year to Rs. 703 crore (US$84.88 million) in 2023 - 2024. India's imports totalled Rs. 72 crore, while its exports totalled Rs. 631 crore. Between April 2024 and February 2025, commerce totalled Rs. 617 crore, of which Rs. 580 crore came from exports and Rs. 36 crore from imports. Improved air connection, such as IndiGo's Mumbai - Mahé flights that began in 2025, has helped Indian companies including Bank of Baroda, Bharti Airtel, Tata Motors, and Ashok Leyland grow in the Seychelles. Solar energy, blue economy initiatives, and financial transparency are all areas of cooperation that build linkages to sustainable development.

india-export-promotion-mission-fta-epc-g:
Summary:

In order to assess the Export Promotion Mission (EPM) and boost India's export growth, Union Commerce and Industry Minister Mr. Piyush Goyal presided over a meeting with Export Promotion Councils (EPCs) and industry associations. A concerted plan to increase exports, investigate new markets, and bolster EPC contributions was the main topic of discussion. The Minister encouraged EPCs to collaborate closely with the Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT) by utilising free trade agreements and providing quantifiable proposals. In order to guarantee quicker, time-bound decisions and minimise delays, the government also instituted a set schedule for important committees. Mr. Rajesh Agarwal, the commerce secretary, emphasised the use of support mechanisms such as logistics assistance, export factoring, and credit guarantees. In order to increase competitiveness, the summit also supported GI-tagged products, District Export Hubs, and ecommerce exports.

Source: IBEF

india-ai-adoption-agentic-ai-consumer-tr:
Summary:

According to Adobe's 2026 AI and Digital Trends Report, India is becoming the Asia Pacific region's most open market for agentic AI, with over 60% of consumers interested in building a personal AI agent. With 55% of respondents wanting to engage with brand AI agents and 58% at ease with agent-to-agent conversation, the study demonstrates a high degree of openness toward the usage of AI. Approximately 61% of people would consent to AI speaking with human agents on their behalf. India exhibits a high level of confidence when it comes to embracing new technologies. 65% of people use AI for personalised suggestions, 60% for customer service, and 62% are open to AI shopping assistants, demonstrating how AI is permeating daily life. Despite productivity benefits, obstacles include data problems, talent gaps, ROI uncertainties, and infrastructure limitations.

Source: IBEF

india-real-estate-investment-2026-growth:
Summary:

Despite inflationary pressures, economic volatility, and geopolitical unpredictability, India's real estate market continued to draw significant institutional investment in the first half of 2026. JLL India reports that from January to June 2026, institutional investment increased 23% year over year to Rs. 40,801 crore (US$4.33 billion), with 54 transactions - the greatest number of half-year deals ever. This comes after a record investment of Rs. 98,942 crore in 2025. With the largest amount ever recorded - Rs. 26,384 crore and 64% of total volumes - domestic investors predominated, countering weaker foreign inflows with the help of private equity funds and REITs. Driven by GCC development and rental yields, the office market led investments with Rs. 21,673 crore across 17 agreements. 34% of capital flows were drawn to Bengaluru and Chennai combined. Investments in 2026 could total between Rs. 80,096 and Rs. 84,807 crore.

Source: IBEF

india-mango-export-singapore-banganapall:
Summary:

The first commercial marine shipment of premium Banganapalle mangoes from India to Singapore was successfully assisted by APEDA, a division of the Ministry of Commerce and Industry, working with ICAR-CISH. On June 11, 2026, Osum Food Solutions LLP exported the 5 MT shipment, which arrived in Singapore on June 24, 2026. The shipment complied with Singapore's quality and phytosanitary criteria after being sourced from GAP-certified orchards in Andhra Pradesh and processed at a packhouse recognised by APEDA in Karnataka. It emphasises sustainable and economical logistics for exports of horticulture products. The export showed excellent post-harvest and cold-chain management, preserving fruit quality while in transit. Farmers profited greatly, receiving roughly Rs. 50 per kg as opposed to local rates of Rs. 25 - 26 per kg, almost doubling returns and enhancing India's export potential.

Source: IBEF

india-new-age-brands-growth-digital-comm:
Summary:

According to Bain & Company and DSG Consumer Partners, India's new-age consumer brands are expanding more than three times faster than the FMCG sector. In FY25, their revenue increased from Rs. 18,942 crore to Rs. 71,033 crore. Digital commerce, fast commerce, and innovation in snacking, cosmetics, personal care, jewellery, home goods, and wearables are the main drivers of growth. These brands outperform larger marketplaces despite having a share of less than 2% because they cater to changing consumer demands. The industries with the biggest increases are jewellery and cosmetics, which are growing 6.5 times and 6 times faster than the market. Protein snacks, healthier drinks, beauty goods with scientific backing, and ergonomic items are how they foster customer loyalty. Scaling is still difficult, with only 22% surpassing Rs. 500 crore and very few surpassing Rs. 100 crore.

Source: IBEF

india-export-growth-msme-fta-global-comp:
Summary:

Union Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal met with industry associations and Export Promotion Councils to discuss ways to boost India's export environment, increase its market share, and boost its competitiveness internationally. Supporting MSMEs, utilising free trade agreements, fostering innovation, developing deep-tech capabilities, enhancing export facilitation, and expanding involvement in global value chains were the main topics of discussion. Industry representatives highlighted initiatives to improve Brand India internationally by sharing sector-wise export performance and progress on key performance indicators. In May 2026, India's exports reached a six-month high of Rs. 4.26 lakh crore, up 18% year over year. There was a trade deficit of Rs. 5.32 lakh crore during April and May of FY27, with exports reaching Rs. 8.38 lakh crore and imports reaching Rs. 13.70 lakh crore.

Source: IBEF

india-us-trade-talks-market-access-suppl:
Summary:

In the face of shifting global trade dynamics, India and the US have advanced talks to improve bilateral trade and expand economic cooperation. In New Delhi, US Trade Representative Ambassador Jamieson Greer met with Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal to discuss current trade talks and discuss potential new areas of cooperation. The proposed bilateral trade agreement, which addresses market access, digital trade, supply chain resilience, lowering non-tariff barriers, and collaboration in key areas, was the main topic of discussion. In order to sustain longterm growth and investment, both sides reiterated their commitment to increasing trade, fostering innovation, and creating reliable supply chains. With robust flows in both directions, India-US goods trade reached Rs. 14.09 lakh crore in 2025. The collaboration seeks to increase investment, market access, and economic resilience.

Source: IBEF

india-steel-sector-digitalisation-ai-iot:
Summary:

In order to increase production, efficiency, and global competitiveness, India's steel industry is being urged to embrace new technologies. Union Steel Minister H.D. Kumaraswamy stated at Chintan Shivir 2026 on Digitalisation in the Steel Sector that intelligent, technologically advanced manufacturing systems - rather than just production capacity—will determine the industry's future. By increasing productivity, streamlining processes, and facilitating data-driven decision-making, technologies like artificial intelligence, machine learning, the industrial internet of things, digital twins, robotics, and advanced data analytics are revolutionising the steel industry. With the help of infrastructural development, Make in India, renewable energy, and urbanisation, the government hopes to reach 300 million tonnes of steel capacity by 2030 and 400 million tonnes by 2035. India's steel competitiveness will be strengthened and difficulties will be addressed with the aid of digitalisation.

Source: IBEF

india-latin-america-trade-growth-2030-in:
Summary:

Due to closer economic links, trade between India and Latin America is expected to treble from Rs. 4.74 lakh crore (US$50 billion) to Rs. 9.47 lakh crore (US$100 billion) by 2030. Alberto Antonio Guani Amarilla, Uruguay's ambassador to India and GRULAC coordinator, stated at the LAC FIRST conference that Latin America will continue to be a vital ally as India advances toward being a developed economy. He outlined significant chances for boosting commerce and investment as well as cooperation. Additionally, he praised India's choice to establish an embassy in Uruguay, describing it as a strengthening of bilateral ties. The ambassador called for the India-Mercosur trade pact to be expanded from 450 to around 3,000 items. Minerals, agro-industry, pharmaceuticals, and renewable energy are important industries.

Source: IBEF

gems-education-india-investment-school-e:
Summary:

The Varkey Group's GEMS Education India intends to increase its footprint in India's education industry by investing Rs. 284.13 crore (US$30 million) over the course of the next three to five years. More than 30 GEMS-operated schools, a network of more than 1,000 GEMS Partner Schools, and a School of Education for teacher preparation, leadership development, and research are all part of the plan. Over three million students will gain from the program, and over 200,000 teachers will have chances. The first five of the 21 school projects that GEMS India has identified will launch in various states in 2026. It is anticipated that the network will employ 10,000 teachers and serve more than 100,000 pupils. Additionally, the business intends to establish an Alternative Investment Fund to aid in the development of school infrastructure.

india-tourism-growth-jobs-2047-economic-:
Summary:

By 2047, it is anticipated that India's tourist and hospitality sector would have grown significantly, adding about 100 million new direct jobs. The Federation of Associations in Indian Tourism and Hospitality (FAITH) estimates that the industry may boost the economy by Rs. 284.13 lakh crore (US$3 trillion) per year, with foreign exchange profits potentially reaching Rs. 42.62 lakh crore (US$450 billion). Puneet Chhatwal stressed the importance of using more effective international marketing and promotions to draw in more foreign visitors. India can use its rich cultural, spiritual, and natural history to achieve sustainable year-round tourist growth with the support of government-led destination development and improved infrastructure, such as airports, highways, and rail links.

Source: IBEF

disclaimer-ibef-content-accuracy-liabili:
Summary:

The PM-KISAN program keeps improving Indian farmers' financial inclusion and income stability. 9.44 crore farmers, including 2.18 crore women, received Rs. 18,880 crore on June 20, 2026, as part of the 23rd installment. Through DBT, the program has disbursed more than Rs. 4.46 lakh crore since 2019, giving qualified families Rs. 6,000 a year for agricultural costs. For ongoing assistance, the Union Budget 2026–2027 set aside Rs. 60,000 crore. Through Aadhaar authentication, e-KYC, online registration, and mobile services, PM-KISAN further advances digital government. Together with programs like AgriStack and Kisan-eMitra, it has strengthened inclusive rural development through technology-driven governance, boosted productivity, improved access to agricultural services, and supported sustainable growth and modernised India's agricultural ecosystem for future resilience.

Source: IBEF

india-video-entertainment-hybrid-model-o:
Summary:

The video entertainment industry in India is changing into a hybrid market where viewers have a say in the production, distribution, and monetisation of content on various platforms. To adapt to shifting customer needs, entertainment organisations are implementing blended revenue models that combine subscriptions, advertising, commerce, and transactions. As a result, a multi-format ecosystem with premium content, creator-led videos, and micro-dramas is now available on mobile devices, connected TVs, and televisions. While increasing the variety of viewing options and monetisation tactics, major platforms such as JioStar, Prime Video, and YouTube are merging technology, commerce, and content. While connected TVs, IPTV, and broadband services continue to gain popularity, mobile devices continue to be the primary screen. In order to remain competitive in the face of divided consumer attention, entertainment organisations are adopting flexible business models and diverse distribution tactics.

Source: IBEF

india-textile-exports-2030-growth-strate:
Summary:

In order to help India reach Rs. 9.47 lakh crore (US$100 billion) in textile and apparel exports by 2030, the Ministry of Textiles has started a statewide effort. In order to establish strategies centred on technological textiles, man-made fibres, infrastructure, logistics, technology, and market access, the two-day summit brings together states, industry leaders, and exporters. State and district export action plans have been created after talks with approximately 200 districts, 36 states, and Union Territories. The program promotes district-led export expansion and cooperative federalism. Giriraj Singh, the minister of textiles, highlighted branding, value addition, diversification, sustainability, and skill. The National Textile Export Roadmap 2030 will direct initiatives to improve global competitiveness and fortify the textile value chain.

Source: IBEF

india-office-reits-growth-fy28-leasable-:
Summary:

Due to asset additions and new REIT listings, it is anticipated that listed commercial office REITs in India will increase their leasable space to 190–195 million square feet by FY28. Strong credit profiles will be maintained by increasing rental revenue, portfolio diversification, and regulated leverage, according to Crisil Ratings. Growth through the purchase of operational assets will guarantee steady cash flows and lower construction risks. It is anticipated that demand from global capability centers, BFSI firms, and flexible workspace operators will maintain occupancy at 92– 93%. Operating margins of about 70% will be supported by contract-based rental increases, and debt levels are probably going to be reasonable at loan-to-value ratios of 26–28%. AI-driven workplace shifts, the slowing economy, and more REIT listings, however, continue to be major obstacles and indicators for the industry going forward.

Source: IBEF

adani-airport-investment-india-aviation-:
Summary:

Adani Group plans to invest Rs. 90,000 crore to Rs. 1 lakh crore across its airport portfolio over the next five years to strengthen India’s aviation infrastructure and connectivity. The investment supports airport expansion, including commercial operations at Mundra Airport with Star Air services linking Kutch to Mumbai and Goa. The group also plans to participate in bidding for 11 new airports. Enhanced connectivity will promote trade, tourism and regional development, with future routes including Ahmedabad. Mundra Airport can accommodate larger aircraft, enabling future growth in passenger traffic. The expansion aligns with the UDAN initiative and is expected to boost India’s aviation ecosystem, infrastructure development and economic growth through sustainable investments and improved regional air transport networks.

Source: IBEF

india-green-housing-market-sustainabilit:
Summary:

India's green housing market is expanding as buyers and developers place a higher priority on affordability, efficiency, and sustainability. According to a Square Yards analysis, green homes can reduce energy expenses by 20–30% and water consumption by 30–50%. Despite the fact that there are more than 19,700 green projects, consumers are unable to compare their sustainability qualities using a uniform system. Square Yards and The Habitat Enterprise developed the Square Yards Green Living Index (SYGLI) to increase transparency. The framework evaluates projects across energy, water, environmental quality, resilience, and mobility factors utilising RERA data, developer disclosures, and geospatial intelligence. Green housing and trustworthy sustainability standards will promote healthier, climate-resilient urban living for the nation's long-term net-zero transition and future, because construction accounts for approximately one-third of India's emissions.

Source: IBEF

india-ship-recycling-global-leader-marit:
Summary:

In 2025, India became the world's top ship recycling country, surpassing its Maritime India Vision 2030 targets. With a volume of 2.99 million GT, the nation's contribution of worldwide recycling increased to 35.4%. 115 yards were able to comply with Hong Kong Convention requirements because to financial assistance, government reforms, and the Recycling of Ships Act of 2019. Recycling and local construction are further encouraged by the Ship-breaking Credit Note Scheme. India wants to increase Alang's capacity to nine million LDT. India is well-positioned to handle 500–600 ships a year, bolstering its position as a sustainable global maritime hub and supporting its long-term objective of maritime leadership and growth. Over 16,000 vessels are anticipated to be recycled globally in the next ten years.

Source: IBEF

indian-railways-kavach-system-safety-tec:
Summary:

At a cost of Rs. 27 crore, Indian Railways has authorised the installation of Kavach, an indigenous Automatic Train Protection system, along 631 route km of the East Coast Railway. As part of Kavach's statewide development through the use of an LTE-based communication network, the project will cover six important railway sections. Through continuous train monitoring and automated braking, the technology enhances railway safety by preventing signal passing at hazard, speeding, and crashes. Additionally, Kavach will improve operating effectiveness, dependability, and timeliness, particularly in inclement weather like heavy fog. In addition to helping Indian Railways modernise its infrastructure, the program will help create a national railway network that is safer, quicker, more effective, and robust for future transportation demands.

Source: IBEF

aadhaar-app-downloads-digital-identity-s:
Summary:

In just five months, the Aadhaar app has been downloaded over 31 million times, demonstrating the growing confidence in digital identity services. It was created by UIDAI and allows users to securely share identity information, download e-Aadhaar, schedule appointments at Aadhaar Seva Kendra, and update mobile numbers and addresses. The software has completed 850,000 address adjustments and nearly 4 million mobile updates. Biometric lock/unlock, facial authentication, authentication history, and QR-based information exchange are some of its features. Hotel checkins, hospital admissions, visitor management, event entry, and gig worker authentication are all supported by the app. By offering safe, easily accessible, and convenient citizen-centric identification services that improve daily governance and digital service delivery across the country, this Android and iOS app fortifies India's digital public infrastructure.

Summary:

With investments in digital infrastructure, artificial intelligence, semiconductors, quantum technology, and sophisticated computing, India is becoming a global leader in technology. 99.9% of districts now have 5G services, while the Digital India initiative has increased internet access and optical fibre networks. Technological self-reliance is being strengthened by significant projects including the National Quantum Mission, IndiaAI Mission, and Semicon India Programme. Over 2,100 Global Capability Centers, extensive AI usage by startups, and 38 supercomputers under the National Supercomputing Mission comprise India's innovation ecosystem. The nation's biotechnology industry has grown to be worth US$190 billion, and its Global Innovation Index ranking rose to 38th in 2025, demonstrating its development as a leader in global innovation and a technological innovator.

maruti-suzuki-iti-manufacturing-labs-guj:
Summary:

Maruti Suzuki India has signed an MoU with Gujarat’s Directorate of Employment and Training to establish five Advanced Manufacturing Labs at ITIs across the state. These labs in Palanpur, Bhavnagar, Surendranagar, Godhra and Dahod will provide hands-on training in modern automotive manufacturing and industry-standard practices. The initiative aims to bridge the gap between technical education and workplace requirements, improving student employability and supporting Gujarat’s industrial growth. Maruti Suzuki currently supports 31 ITIs and 18 Advanced Manufacturing Labs nationwide in manufacturing-related trades. With the addition of five new labs, its total AMLs will rise to 23 across seven states and Union Territories. The initiative will strengthen industry-academia collaboration and create a future-ready workforce for India's manufacturing ambitions.

adb-investment-india-private-sector-gree:
Summary:

India continues to be the largest market for private sector activities of the Asian Development Bank, which intends to provide development-aligned projects with direct financing of around US$1 billion (Rs 9,436 crore) in 2026. It intends to keep up the momentum from 2025, when it committed more than US$4 billion in sovereign finance and US$1 billion in private sector investment, while raising comparable external funds to achieve almost US$2 billion in total private flows. Renewable energy, green hydrogen, electric vehicles, sustainable infrastructure, green data centers, aiding the climate transition, energy security, and long-term competitiveness are important investment sectors. ADB aims to mobilise private finance and bolster confidence in India's growth and green transition, while India also receives help for renewable energy, urban development, logistics, transportation, and skills development. optimistic outlook.

india-exports-growth-global-resilience-t:
Summary:

Despite tariff pressures and worldwide concerns, India's product exports demonstrated remarkable resilience. According to Union Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal, exports increased by almost 15% year over year from April 1 and June 14, 2026, demonstrating the power of commerce in the face of global problems. Exports reached a six-month high of Rs. 4.26 lakh crore (US$45.2 billion) in May 2026, an 18% increase over the previous year. Exports jumped 16.09% to Rs. 8.39 lakh crore in April and May of 2026, while imports increased 15.14% to Rs. 13.71 lakh crore, resulting in a Rs. 5.33 lakh crore trade imbalance. India is still optimistic that its exports would hit $1 trillion in FY27. The goal of trade negotiations with the US on June 23–24, 2026, is to enhance market access and lessen tariff concerns in order to promote sustained export growth.

Source: IBEF

india-defence-manufacturing-growth-expor:
Summary:

Strong industrial growth was demonstrated by India's domestic defence output, which increased from Rs. 46,000 crore (US$7.54 billion) in 2014 to over Rs. 1,78,000 crore (US$20.42 billion) in FY25. Due to the growing demand for Indian-made equipment worldwide, defence exports rose from Rs. 1,000 crore prior to 2014 to around Rs. 40,000 crore. military Minister Rajnath Singh emphasised independence in military manufacture by laying the groundwork for a 10,000-ton aluminium extrusion press at Yantra India Limited in Nagpur. The plant would serve railways, ISRO, and DRDO while producing aluminium alloy extrusions for the defence, aerospace, and key industries. It boosts the growth of indigenous industry while lowering imports.

india-uk-ceta-ai-technology-innovation-t:
Summary:

In addition to potential under the India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA), India and the UK have talked about strengthening cooperation in artificial intelligence, new technologies, research, and innovation. In order to promote shared economic growth and closer bilateral ties, discussions between UK Permanent Secretary Emran Mian and India's High Commissioner to the UK P. Kumaran concentrated on bolstering technology collaborations, innovation ecosystems, and cooperative research. The talks focused on how to use CETA to open up new commercial, research, and innovation opportunities in high-growth industries including artificial intelligence and cutting-edge technologies. The agreement is anticipated to promote MSMEs, startups, and long-term innovation-led development while expanding trade, investment, and market access ahead of the implementation of CETA and the Double Contribution Convention (DCC) on July 15, 2026.

reliance-ai-strategy-india-ai-backbone-d:
Summary:

In order to establish India as a global leader in AI-driven innovation and to construct the nation's independent AI backbone, Reliance Industries has unveiled an ambitious artificial intelligence plan. To serve cutting-edge applications across industries, the company intends to create one of the biggest AI computing platforms in the world. By making significant investments in cutting-edge technology and facilitating broader access to AI tools for companies, developers, and consumers, this effort aims to boost India's digital capabilities. Reliance also unveiled AI-powered services, such as an AI-driven home platform to incorporate smart services into homes and the Jio AI Call Agent for intelligent voice conversations. Together, these initiatives seek to boost digital transformation, increase the application of AI across industries, and bolster India's innovation environment and technological independence.

aleta-planet-india-entry-cross-border-pa:
Summary:

As part of its regional expansion plan in cross-border and multi-currency payments, Singaporebased fintech company Aleta Planet is entering the Indian market. In order to expedite the introduction of card programs for financial institutions, the business intends to collaborate with licensed Indian entities to develop plug-and-play infrastructure for Visa debit card issuance, payments, and compliance. Because of its strong economic ties to Singapore and the Middle East, as well as its quickly expanding digital payments market, Aleta views India as a significant possibility. Additionally, a senior director has been appointed to oversee partnerships with an emphasis on India. By partnering with Visa and Sanrio to introduce themed debit and prepaid cards with characters like Hello Kitty, the company hopes to duplicate its Singapore model. On June 22, 2026, about 9,000 cards will be made available.

india-ci-energy-storage-market-growth-re:
Summary:

IESA projects that the C&I energy storage market in India will grow from less than 1 GWh in 2025 to 22–31 GWh by 2032. Rising power prices, the use of renewable energy, dependability requirements, and decarbonisation objectives are what propel growth. Energy optimisation, resilience, and cost savings are replacing backup applications in the market. Capacity could reach 22–23 GWh under the BAU scenario, but quick adoption could raise it to 31 GWh. Lead-acid, lithium-ion, sodium-ion, vanadium-redox flow, and pumped hydro systems are examples of storage technologies. Energy storage is a crucial component of India's clean energy transition, with significant potential for long-term sustainable industrial growth ahead, thanks to improving economics, expanding renewable integration, and regulatory backing.

modi-paris-visit-india-france-relations-:
Summary:

During his visit to Paris following the G7 Summit, Prime Minister Narendra Modi commended the Indian diaspora in France for improving India-France relations. He emphasised their function in fostering tighter interpersonal relationships, economic involvement, and cultural bonds. Participating in VivaTech 2026, which showcases India's digital economy, startups, and developing technologies, is another aspect of the tour. In the areas of military, marine security, renewable energy, artificial intelligence, innovation, and sophisticated manufacturing, France and India are still expanding their collaboration. Bilateral ties have been further strengthened by initiatives like the India-France Innovation Roadmap 2030 and collaborations in research, technology, and skilling. The visit highlights how crucial innovation-driven cooperation and the diaspora will be to furthering the strategic alliance between both countries in the next years.

india-pharma-industry-growth-affordable-:
Summary:

Over the past 12 years, India's pharmaceutical industry has undergone substantial change, resulting in increased access to healthcare, more reasonably priced medications, and indigenous manufacture. The nation is now the third-largest pharmaceutical manufacturer in the world by volume and a significant provider of generic medications to more than 200 nations and territories. Through more than 15,000 Janaushadhi Kendras, government programs including the PLI Scheme, Bulk Drug Parks, and PMBJP have increased manufacturing and increased access to reasonably priced medications. Complex generics and biosimilars are now more capable thanks to increased investments in R&D, biotechnology, vaccines, and infrastructure. India's status as the world's pharmacy was strengthened by its involvement in the COVID-19 epidemic. In the upcoming years, exports, employment, and long-term sector growth will be supported by a sustained emphasis on innovation, reforms, and self-reliance.

india-rural-roads-pmgsy-fy27-connectivit:
Summary:

The Centre has targeted construction of 26,474 km of rural roads in FY27 with an allocation of Rs. 18,907 crore under PMGSY and related schemes. A review meeting led by Rural Development Secretary Rohit Kansal assessed progress under PMGSY and RCPLWEA, emphasising faster completion of unconnected habitations, particularly in remote and tribal regions. The Government prioritised connectivity for Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups through improved DPR preparation, timely land acquisition and stronger coordination among agencies. Focus was also placed on completing projects in Maoist-affected areas, maintaining infrastructure quality and ensuring effective road upkeep. Wider adoption of the e-MARG platform will enhance transparency, monitoring, payment tracking and efficiency, supporting reliable all-weather rural connectivity across India.

brics-msme-forum-agra-india-innovation-s:
Summary:

The First BRICS MSME Forum and the Third SME Working Group Meeting were held in Agra by the Ministry of MSME with the topic "Building for Resilience, Innovation, Cooperation and Sustainability." In order to fortify MSME ecosystems, advance sustainability, and strengthen international cooperation, the event brought together policymakers, business executives, and entrepreneurs from BRICS and partner countries. With more than 8.6 crore businesses, the MSME sector in India accounts for 31.1% of GDP, 35.4% of manufacturing output, and 48.58% of exports. The program included talks and exhibits on sustainable growth, technology adoption, and finance. In order to build competitive, future-ready MSMEs, delegates shared best practices and policy experiences. This strengthened BRICS cooperation and revealed new avenues for innovation, entrepreneurship, and global market integration.

nykaa-fy30-gmv-growth-ai-beauty-fashion-:
Summary:

By FY30, Nykaa hopes to reach GMV of Rs. 47,635 crore (US$5 billion), propelled by growing consumer spending and expansion in the beauty and fashion sectors. The company anticipates a two-to three-fold increase in sales, a four-to five-fold increase in EBITDA, and an improvement in margins to low-to-mid teens. It intends to greatly increase its beauty and fashion industries and build its retail network to more than 600 locations. Through more than 40 GenAI projects, such as automated advertising, personalised suggestions, and AI-powered customer service, Nykaa is also speeding up digital transformation. Long-term growth in India's growing beauty and lifestyle retail sector is anticipated to be supported by its omnichannel strategy, rise of owned brands, and increased fashion profitability.

india-agriculture-exports-growth-global-:
Summary:

According to APEDA Chairman Abhishek Dev, India's agricultural export basket has grown dramatically over the last ten years, from roughly 280 products to nearly 500 products. Last year, exports reached approximately Rs 4.61 lakh crore (US$53 billion), maintaining India's position among the top ten agricultural exporters in the world despite competition from the US, Brazil, China, and the EU. The growth is a result of initiatives to increase market access, diversify exports, and boost the agriculture sector's competitiveness in international markets. Value-added and indemand goods including pulses, cocoa products, pineapples, grapes, and pomegranates are now among India's agricultural exports. These goods are sold in numerous foreign markets, demonstrating the increasing popularity of Indian agricultural products around the world and promoting farmers' livelihoods and sustainable rural development.

india-solar-agriculture-renewable-farmin:
Summary:

Particularly in Africa, India's agricultural revolution driven by renewable energy is becoming recognised globally as a paradigm for sustainable development. Through programs like PMKUSUM and the International Solar Alliance, it emphasises how solar-powered farming can improve rural livelihoods, farmer incomes, and food security. India demonstrates how renewable energy may fill up irrigation shortages, especially in Sub-Saharan Africa, where irrigation coverage is far lower than in India. Better crop output, less reliance on diesel, and cheaper irrigation are all supported by the model. Millions of solar pumps have been made possible by PM-KUSUM, and more are anticipated by 2030. The goal of Mission 300 is to grow solar agriculture for power. India is establishing itself as a global leader in clean energy integration and sustainable agriculture thanks to its quick expansion of solar and renewable energy capacity.

india-electronics-manufacturing-exports-:
Summary:

Driven by increased production, exports, and regulatory backing, India is quickly solidifying its place as a global center for electronics manufacturing. Speaking during the opening of the Jabil Pune facility, Ashwini Vaishnaw stated that India is becoming a popular manufacturing site and the sixth-largest exporter of electronics. India seeks to become second-largest exporter through investment in manufacturing and supply chains. With two factories currently under construction and two more planned, the government is increasing semiconductor capacity. Pune is developing precision manufacturing. With a robust infrastructure and around 60% of data center capacity, Maharashtra is an important hub. Large-scale initiatives like Wadhwan Port and the MumbaiAhmedabad Bullet Train will improve commerce, growth, and logistics. India's place in the global electronics manufacturing and export ecosystem growth is strengthened by this.
