With the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding between AYUSHEXCIL and the Spices Board of India in front of Union Minister Prataprao Jadhav, India bolstered its aspirations for global wellness. Through export promotion, quality control, research, innovation, and market development for nutraceuticals, herbal extracts, and Ayurvedic goods, the collaboration seeks to promote Ayush products and medicinal spices worldwide. The partnership supports farmers, exporters, MSMEs, startups, and women entrepreneurs while working to improve scientific validation, traceability, and global competitiveness. india-nano-fertiliser-agritech-innovatio:
Summary:

In order to grow its nano-fertilizer business, German agritech company B+H Solutions GmbH intends to invest Rs. 11.12 crore in India in 2026. This move is motivated by the growing need for metal-based agricultural inputs and the growing acceptance of nanotechnology among Indian farmers. The company, which operates as Dr. Heinisch Agro Solutions India Private Limited, recently obtained FCO registration for its AgroCopper nano-copper product and is currently requesting approval for nano iron solutions. AgroBeize, which was created especially for Indian agriculture, is one of the eight products that B+H presently sells in India. Copper, silver, and trace elements are used in its nanotechnology portfolio to increase agricultural yields, plant immunity, and nutrient efficiency. The company intends to expand throughout Indian states through distributors and internal teams, claiming yield advantages of up to 30%.

prism-us-expansion-india-global-hospital:
Summary:

The parent company of Oyo, PRISM, has increased its foothold in the US hospitality market by opening 38 extended-stay hotels run by G6 Hospitality under the Studio 6 brand. Targeting corporate professionals, relocating families, and long-term project workers looking for reasonably priced accommodations with hotel-style amenities, the properties are dispersed throughout major cities like New York, Dallas, Houston, and Phoenix. The majority of hotels have between 100 and 120 rooms with workstations, laundry rooms, internet access, kitchenettes, and flexible housekeeping services. Following the earlier opening of eight Studio 6 hotels this year, the expansion underscores the rising demand for affordable extended-stay lodging. To enhance operations, PRISM also intends to implement AI-driven systems, revenue management tools, and technology created in India. The action complements the company's larger aspirations for worldwide expansion while fortifying its position in the US market.

india-cement-sector-growth-fy27-cost-pre:
Summary:

India’s cement makers expect FY27 to be a healthy growth year, projecting 7- 8% volume growth despite geopolitical uncertainty in West Asia and rising fuel costs. Demand is supported by strong government infrastructure spending, housing activity and ongoing urbanisation. Major companies including UltraTech Cement, Ambuja Cements, Shree Cement, Dalmia Cement and Nuvoco Vistas remain optimistic about medium-term growth prospects. Firms are also focusing on premiumisation and improving trade mix to enhance realisations, even as they face higher input costs linked to crude oil-driven freight, packing and supply chain pressures. Industry players are continuing expansion and efficiency investments. Ambuja Cements targets about 80 million tonnes sales in FY27, while Dalmia Bharat has planned ₹3,200 - 3,400 crore capex. Nuvoco Vistas has allocated ₹900 crore for expansion, and UltraTech Cement remains focused on capacity growth and leadership. Despite short-term cost pressures, strong domestic demand and infrastructure development are expected to sustain growth momentum in FY27.

bhavya-industrial-parks-india-manufactur:
Summary:

Operational instructions for the Bharat Audyogik Vikas Yojna (BHAVYA), a project to build 100 top-notch industrial parks around India, have been announced by the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT). The Union Cabinet approved the plan, which will cost ₹33,660 crore (US$3.53 billion) between FY27 and FY32. By building investment-ready hubs with plug and-play infrastructure, multimodal logistics, dependable utilities, digital governance systems, and sustainable characteristics, it seeks to fortify India's manufacturing environment. A competitive approach based on infrastructure, connectivity, ecological strength, and sustainability will be used in phase one to choose up to 50 parks. It will be executed through SPVs with NICDC as the project management agency, supporting investment, employment, and industrial expansion throughout India. It is in line with Make in India and PM Gati Shakti.

foreign-company-registrations-india-fy26:
Summary:

Strong international confidence in India's economy is reflected in the country's foreign business registrations, which reached a nine-year high in FY26 (101 compared to 57 in FY25). With 13 registrations, Singapore was the top new entrant, followed by the US with 10 and the UK with 9. Other notable contributors included South Korea, Germany, and Japan. Over half of all registrations came from these six nations, indicating a concentrated but expanding global interest. South Africa, Ghana, and Uzbekistan made their maiden entries, while South Korea and Germany saw significant rises. The trend emphasises India's growing significance in international supply networks, investment flows, and its allure as a major hub for manufacturing and consumption.

india-electrical-equipment-industry-glob:
Summary:

According to McKinsey & Company's "Wired for Growth" report, India's electrical equipment business is expected to grow quickly over the next ten years due to increased domestic demand, electrification, the deployment of renewable energy, and export prospects. Exports could surpass Rs. 5.52 lakh crore (US$60 billion), while domestic output could increase from Rs. 4.42 lakh crore (US$50 billion) in 2025 to Rs. 17.94-21.62 lakh crore (US$195-235 billion) by 2035. Investments in batteries, power infrastructure, and industrial modernisation are predicted to propel the industry's 11–13% annual growth. Growing reliance on imports is still a problem, though. In order to boost competitiveness and lessen dependency on imports across important electrical equipment manufacturing areas, McKinsey suggested increasing domestic production capacity and localisation.

Source: IBEF

india-us-economic-cooperation-global-eco:
Summary:

According to Union Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal, trust, common interests, and close interpersonal relationships make India and the US natural partners. He emphasised growing collaboration in commerce, investment, technology, and strategic areas while speaking to the Indo American Chamber of Commerce in New Delhi. He pointed out that India's standing as a destination for international investment has been reinforced by reforms, digitisation, and infrastructural development. According to Goyal, India's economy has moved from being one of the "Fragile Five" to one of the top five in the world, and it may soon rise to the third position. Additionally, he emphasised cooperation in sophisticated manufacturing, quantum computing, artificial intelligence, and GCCs. He added that India's economy might reach US$30–35 trillion by 2047.

Source: IBEF

india-nordic-partnership-green-technolog:
Summary:

The 3rd India-Nordic Summit in Oslo marked a major shift in India’s engagement with Nordic nations through a Green Technology and Innovation Strategic Partnership. Leaders from India, Denmark, Norway, Finland, Iceland and Sweden discussed cooperation in trade, digitalisation, artificial intelligence, climate action, energy security, healthcare, space and defence. The summit coincided with progress in the India-EU Free Trade Agreement and implementation of the India EFTA Trade and Economic Partnership Agreement. The joint statement supported resilient supply chains, WTO-led trade governance and collaboration in AI, 6G, Arctic research and maritime security. EFTA countries also proposed US$ 100 billion investment in India, potentially creating one million direct jobs.

Source: IBEF

india-chemical-sector-industrial-growth-:
Summary:

According to Nivedita Shukla Verma, India's chemical industry has the potential to become a significant engine of industrial growth and create about 10 million employment by 2040. According to her, the industry is making a substantial contribution to manufacturing, exports, and economic growth thanks to growing domestic demand and increased interest from around the world in India as a potential location for manufacturing. Important sectors like consumer products, agribusiness, textiles, medicines, and automobiles are supported by this sector. India, one of the biggest manufacturers and users of chemicals worldwide, is anticipated to gain from investments in sustainable manufacturing, regulatory assistance, and supply-chain diversification. In order to boost competitiveness, fortify industrial ecosystems, and support India's aspirations for a developed economy by 2047, Verma emphasised the significance of innovation, research, cutting-edge technologies, and circular economy practices.

Source: IBEF

india-measurement-reforms-fair-trade-glo:
Summary:

India has introduced reforms to strengthen measurement infrastructure and modernise the country’s legal metrology ecosystem on World Metrology Day 2026. The Department of Consumer Affairs announced measures to improve accuracy, transparency and efficiency in measurement systems used across trade, industry and public services. Key initiatives include expansion of the eMaap Portal, stronger verification and certification systems, and progress under the ‘One Nation, One Time’ initiative for unified national timekeeping. The government said legal metrology is essential for fair trade, consumer protection and ease of doing business. India has also strengthened standards certification and measurement science under the OIML framework. These reforms are expected to support industrial growth, regulatory compliance, innovation and India’s global competitiveness in coming years.

Source: IBEF

uber-jsw-partnership-india-electric-mobi:
Summary:

In order to promote sustainable mobility and greener transportation, Uber and JSW Group have teamed to deploy electric vehicles throughout India's ride-hailing sector. As part of the partnership, JSW Group will make it easier for EVs to be supplied to Uber's platform, allowing driver partners to embrace them more widely. The project is anticipated to accelerate the electrification of urban transit, cut operating costs, and reduce carbon emissions. Uber's sustainability objectives and India's drive for electric mobility through investments in clean transportation, infrastructure for charging, and supportive regulations are all in line with the cooperation. Additionally, it shows the increasing momentum in India's EV ecosystem, which includes investments in fleet electrification, battery technology, and the integration of renewable energy. According to industry experts, these kinds of partnerships can help India achieve its long-term climate and energy transition goals, enhance green infrastructure, and improve urban air quality.

Source: IBEF

india-toffee-exports-growth-made-in-indi:
Summary:

India’s exports of toffees, caramels and similar confectionery products have grown over the past decade, reflecting rising global demand for Indian brands. Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal noted exports increased by 166%, from Rs. 49.68 crore in FY2013-14 to Rs. 132 crore in FY2025-26, calling it a “toffee tale” that highlights the strength of Made in India products and expanding FMCG exports driven by better quality, branding and market access.

During Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s to Rome, he gifted Melody toffees to Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni, drawing public attention and symbolizing India’s abroad. boosted interest in Indian products, with experts suggesting stronger export opportunities across confectionery and packaged foods due to rising global awareness.

india-italy-strategic-partnership-tech-d:
Summary:

India-Italy strategic cooperation in commerce, technology, defence, clean energy, and connectivity would be strengthened, according to a common vision presented by Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni and Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi. They reaffirmed their commitment to the 2024 Joint Strategic Action Plan and emphasised the growing convergence on global and economic challenges in a joint Op-Ed. They observed growing bilateral relations bolstered by corporate cooperation, political involvement, and interpersonal interactions. In AI, semiconductors, clean technologies, and advanced manufacturing, they placed a strong emphasis on innovation, research, and industry collaboration. It is anticipated that the collaboration will improve supply chains, investment flows, and industrial cooperation in key industries. Additionally, they emphasised the significance of the IMEC corridor for regional economic cooperation, commercial integration, and connectivity. They reiterated their commitment to sustainability and rules-based order.

india-digital-economy-20-percent-gdp-203:
Summary:

NCIIPC DG M. U. Nair projects that by 2030, India's digital economy will account for 20% of GDP. He emphasised how internet usage, digital public infrastructure, and emerging technologies are driving rapid expansion. Aadhaar, UPI, and DPI are examples of initiatives that have changed how people and businesses interact. India's digital leadership is strengthened by the opportunities this digital expansion brings for investment, entrepreneurship, and innovation. He emphasised that resilience and sustained growth depend on cybersecurity. In order to combat cyber dangers, he urged cooperation between the government, business, and industry. Digital transformation and value creation are being accelerated by AI, cloud, IoT, and startups. This helps India achieve its objective of having an inclusive, multitrillion-dollar digital economy.

india-metrology-system-fair-trade-qualit:
Summary:

Through a cutting-edge regulatory framework, India's developing metrology ecosystem is bolstering fair trade, consumer protection, industrial quality, and global competitiveness. In trade and industry, the Legal Metrology Act of 2009 guarantees precision and dependability in weights and measures. Government programs like "One Nation, One Time" and the eMaap Portal increase efficiency, transparency, and simplicity of conducting business. Through greater infrastructure and foreign certifications, India has also improved its standing internationally, increasing customer confidence and conforming to international standards. By guaranteeing uniform measurements, the system helps industries like manufacturing, healthcare, telecom, and energy. Digital governance, OIML certification, and system synchronisation are the main areas of reform that enable trade expansion, industrial growth, and global competitiveness.

bhim-app-upi-growth-india-digital-paymen:
Summary:

The fast growth of India's digital payments ecosystem and increasing UPI usage are reflected in the BHIM (Bharat Interface for Money) app's transaction volumes, which increased by more than 300% in FY26. As a result of increased smartphone use and financial inclusion, BHIM saw significant increases in users, retailers, and transaction value, according to NPCI. This increase demonstrates how both urban and rural areas are moving toward cashless transactions. BHIM supports India's ambition for digital public infrastructure and facilitates smooth peer-to-peer and merchant transactions via UPI. Due to regulatory backing, fintech innovation, and internet access, India's payments ecosystem is growing. Through safe, digital transactions, UPI has emerged as a major real-time payment system that promotes financial inclusion, transparency, and economic formalisation across the country.

southwest-airlines-hyderabad-innovation-:
Summary:

Southwest Airlines, a US-based airline, has established its first overseas innovation center in Hyderabad, which is a significant milestone in the airline's global expansion and bolsters India's standing as a center for innovation and technology. With an emphasis on software engineering, data science, artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, and digital transformation, the Global Capability Centre (GCC) is anticipated to eventually employ about 1,000 people. Strong international trust in India's highly qualified labour force, thriving digital environment, and developing technological infrastructure is reflected in the center. Multinational corporations in the fields of aviation, IT, healthcare, and finance are still drawn to Hyderabad. The facility will facilitate enhanced capability building, knowledge transfer, and employment generation. India is becoming a popular location for international innovation, research, and technology-driven business transformation, with more than 1,800 GCCs.

upi-dominance-india-digital-payments-gro:
Summary:

According to RBI data, UPI accounted for 85.5% of all digital payment transactions in India during the second half of 2025, indicating a robust expansion of the country's digital payments ecosystem. UPI's supremacy was attributed by the RBI's Payment Systems Report to growing smartphone usage, smooth transactions, and developing digital infrastructure. Due to its emphasis on high-value transactions, RTGS held 0.1% of the transaction volume, while NEFT and PPIs each contributed 3.6%. However, RTGS had the highest transaction value (68.6%), followed by NEFT (14.9%) and UPI (9.5%). Over the course of the last ten years of digitisation, public faith in safe and cashless payment systems has grown throughout India, as seen by the 33-fold growth in digital payment volumes and nearly threefold increase in transaction values between 2016 and 2025.

Source: IBEF

india-norway-tepa-trade-green-energy-inv:
Summary:

During the India-Norway Business and Research Summit in Oslo, which was attended by Crown Prince Haakon, Norwegian Prime Minister Jonas Gahr Støre, and Prime Minister Narendra Modi, the two countries deepened their commercial ties. Over 250 people attended the event, including CEOs from over 50 businesses. The India-EFTA Trade and Economic Partnership Agreement (TEPA), which aims to create one million jobs in India and investments totalling Rs. 9.43 lakh crore (US$100 billion), was the main topic of discussion. Both nations investigated collaboration in the areas of digital infrastructure, vital minerals, healthcare, shipbuilding, renewable energy, and startups. Additionally, cooperation in green hydrogen, maritime decarbonisation, climate finance, sustainability, and technologies in line with India's Viksit Bharat vision was emphasised in the updated Green Strategic Partnership.

india-reforms-growth-viksit-bharat-2047-:
Summary:

Union Minister Piyush Goyal stated at the ASSOCHAM India Business Reform Summit 2026 that India is using global geopolitical uncertainty to propel economic reforms, fortify supply networks, and quicken the country's progress towards Viksit Bharat 2047. He emphasised increased industrygovernment cooperation to boost competitiveness and ease of doing business. He highlighted the interruptions caused by COVID-19, the conflict in Ukraine, and the West Asian crisis while highlighting India's capacity to use governance and reforms to turn obstacles into opportunities. Approximately 2 million direct and 10 million indirect employment are presently produced by India's 1,800 Global Capability Centers. Growth is anticipated to be fuelled by emerging industries like artificial intelligence, cyber security, cloud computing, and data centers. Despite recent global uncertainty, India's exports hit a record Rs. 74.62 lakh crore.

Source: IBEF

india-agri-reforms-digital-farming-ai-go:
Summary:

In order to streamline governance, increase transparency, and hasten the agri-sector's digital revolution, India has implemented significant agricultural reforms. The "Reform Express" project, which was announced by Agriculture Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan, aims to simplify clearances for farmers, merchants, and retailers while reducing paperwork. The licensing procedure for household insecticides has been streamlined, with QR codes taking the place of traditional inserts and application forms being reduced from three pages to one. Eliminating dual approvals has also made it easier to register fertilisers under the Fertiliser Control Order. Additionally, PQMS and ICEGATE have digitally integrated all 649 customs ports for single-window import applications. Through integrated digital agricultural services, the AI-powered Bharat-VISTAAR platform has now responded to over 44 lakh farmer enquiries across the country.

Source: IBEF

team-initiative-msme-digital-growth-ondc:
Summary:

Through the Trade Enablement and Access to Market (TEAM) Initiative of the Ministry of MSME, which is a component of the World Bank-backed RAMP Programme, India is quickening the digital transformation of its micro and small businesses. The program, run by the National Small Industries Corporation, offers end-to-end e-commerce support by integrating small firms with the Open Network for Digital Commerce ecosystem. It assists local manufacturers, small merchants, homebased business owners, and craftsmen with onboarding, cataloguing, packaging, logistics, and customer service. It encourages equitable digital growth and broader market access by reducing commission costs and entry obstacles. With Baba Sankari FPO in Udhampur increasing walnut sales nationally and Kanglei Foods in Manipur receiving regular interstate orders thanks to enhanced logistics and doorstep delivery assistance, the initiative has demonstrated a significant impact in rural areas.

pepsico-india-investment-manufacturing-e:
Summary:

By 2030, PepsiCo India plans to invest up to Rs. 5,700 crore (US$591 million) to increase the manufacturing capacity of its food division throughout India. According to CEO Jagrut Kotecha, the investment will primarily go toward three significant facilities in Madhya Pradesh, Assam, and Tamil Nadu. The company wants to expand its supply chain network and increase manufacturing capacity in order to satisfy growing consumer demand. PepsiCo India announced revenue of Rs. 9,789 crore and profit after tax of Rs. 905 crore for the year ended December 2025, demonstrating consistent financial performance. The development complements PepsiCo's strategy of growing plants, enhancing regional manufacturing, and emphasising innovation and sustainability in operations. It also demonstrates the company's strong confidence in India as a major growth market.

india-export-target-1-trillion-fy27-atma:
Summary:

Under Atmanirbhar Bharat, India hopes to export Rs. 96.30 lakh crore (US$1 trillion) in 2026–2027 and Rs. 192.60 lakh crore (US$2 trillion) in five years, according to Union Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal. He pointed out that despite global difficulties, exports increased by almost 5% to Rs. 75.94 lakh crore in 2025–2026. With free trade agreements (FTAs) with 38 industrialised nations expanding market access, he emphasised exporting as a national purpose. In order to promote and substitute Swadeshi manufacture, he called for import surveillance. He emphasised increased domestic demand, more competition, and the involvement of young people and startups. The government is enhancing trade infrastructure, logistics, and business ease. Merchandise exports totalled Rs. 38.88 crore, while services exports totalled Rs. 37.08 crore.

Summary:

According to Prime Minister Narendra Modi, the India-EU Free commerce Agreement will improve India's standing as a reliable partner for Europe by creating new opportunities in commerce, technology, manufacturing, and robust supply chains. Speaking at the European Round Table for Industry in Gothenburg, Sweden, he emphasised the significance of reliable alliances in an unpredictable world and the increasing strategic convergence. According to him, connectivity projects like the India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor will strengthen economic connections and foster greater cooperation. He presented India as a desirable location for investment because of its development, reforms, digital public infrastructure, and growing infrastructure. He gathered European businesses and restated the goals of Design for India, Make in India, and Export from India. Together, leaders talked about collaboration in important strategic areas.

india-gdp-growth-forecast-morgan-stanley:
Summary:

According to Morgan Stanley, strong domestic demand, regulatory changes, and increased investment in manufacturing and infrastructure would propel India's economy despite global uncertainty. India's GDP is expected to rise by 6.7% in FY27, making it one of the fastest-growing economies. Stronger rural demand, increased government capital expenditures, declining inflation, and a resurgence of private investment were cited in the report as important sources of support. It highlighted the advantages of expanding digital-physical infrastructure and diversifying supply chains, citing industries including electronics, defence, renewable energy, and semiconductors as long-term drivers. India's fundamentals are strong despite global concerns, oil volatility, and geopolitical tensions. Reforms, financial stability, and policy continuity are improving investor confidence and growth prospects.

india-hotel-investments-growth-q1-2026-t:
Summary:

According to JLL, investments in India's hotel industry totalled Rs. 1,635.94 crore (US$185 million) between January and March 2026, a 58% increase from the previous year. This contrasts with Rs. 989.36 crore during the same time last year, indicating high investor confidence fuelled by increased demand for travel, improved occupancy, and growing branded hotel infrastructure. Additionally, the industry continued to grow after 2025, when investments reached Rs. 5,010.88 crore, a 67% increase. Recent transactions included land monetisation, operating hotel investments, and acquisitions. For example, Warburg Pincus committed Rs. 945.62 crore to purchase a 41% share in Fleur Hotels. Due to robust liquidity, private equity interest, and land prospects connected to airports, JLL anticipates continuous expansion. Investments were concentrated on Tier-I cities, and branded hotel openings and signings rose dramatically throughout India.

ntpc-capacity-90gw-solar-growth-clean-en:
Summary:

After NTPC Renewable Energy Ltd. commissioned an additional 110.25 MW of solar power at the Khavda Solar Energy Project in Gujarat through its subsidiary NGEL, the NTPC Group's installed capacity surpassed 90 GW. With this addition, NTPC's overall capacity increased to 90,025 MW, solidifying its standing as the biggest integrated power utility in India. The development shows NTPC's increasing emphasis on renewables and is a significant turning point in India's clean energy transition. One of the biggest solar parks in the world is anticipated to be the Khavda Renewable Energy Park in Gujarat. With a goal of 60 GW of renewable capacity by 2032, NTPC is growing its solar, wind, pumped hydro, and green hydrogen projects. Investments in green infrastructure, increased demand, and government backing are propelling capacity expansion, bolstering energy security, and advancing net-zero objectives.

maruti-suzuki-kharkhoda-plant-production:
Summary:

In response to growing domestic and international demand, Maruti Suzuki India Ltd. has increased capacity by beginning commercial production at its second manufacturing facility in Kharkhoda, Haryana. The new plant would manufacture passenger cars for the Indian and international markets, with an annual manufacturing capacity of 2.5 lakh units. It is a component of the company's longterm growth plan, which aims to increase production flexibility, supply chain integration, and efficiency. Maruti Suzuki is increasing its manufacturing footprint with this acquisition in order to satisfy the rising demand for automobiles. With its cutting-edge automation and environmentally friendly production techniques, the factory is anticipated to create a large number of jobs in Haryana. In order to help India's car growth, exports, and industrial ecosystem development, the business intends to gradually increase Kharkhoda's capacity to 10 lakh units per year.

india-netherlands-deals-semiconductors-c:
Summary:

During PM Narendra Modi's visit, India and the Netherlands inked 17 agreements that strengthened their collaboration in semiconductors, strategic technologies, education, clean energy, healthcare, agriculture, and migration. The India-Netherlands Strategic Partnership Roadmap 2026-2030 was approved by both parties to direct international cooperation, trade, and innovation. Important Memorandums of Understanding include crucial minerals cooperation between ministries and Tata Electronics and ASML for semiconductor production in Gujarat. Both countries agreed to facilitate migration, and the Netherlands will restore Chola copper plates to India. Additionally, collaboration grew in the areas of green hydrogen, renewable energy, and water management, including the Kalpasar project. There were announcements of new centers of excellence in dairy (Bengaluru) and floriculture (Tripura). Sustainable and manufacturing relationships are further strengthened by collaborations between ICMR, Dutch organisations, universities, and customs.

Source: IBEF

india-fdi-growth-fta-reforms-adb-outlook:
Summary:

FDI inflows will increase as a result of India's extension of free trade agreements, tariff rationalisation, and enhanced business environment, according to ADB Chief Economist Albert Park. Net FDI increased to US$ 3 billion in April–December of FY26 after declining from Rs. 2.88 crore (US$ 38.6 billion) in FY22 to Rs. 2.25 crore in FY23 and Rs. 84,435 crore in FY24. Competitiveness is anticipated to increase with trade liberalisation, supply chain integration, and improvements to the manufacturing ecosystem. Labour, GST, and urban governance reforms make conducting business easier and draw in investors. Tensions in the Middle East and oil prices, which are expected to reach US$96 in 2026 and US$80 in 2027, are risks. Despite this, domestic demand and reforms are expected to maintain India's GDP growth of 6.9%.

Source: IBEF

india-urban-infrastructure-investment-20:
Summary:

According to Brickwork Ratings, India would require around Rs. 80 lakh crore (US$854.43 billion) in urban infrastructure investment by 2037 to accommodate fast urbanisation and economic growth. By 2036, urban areas are predicted to account for almost 70% of India's GDP, making finance for sustainable cities a top issue. In an effort to transition urban development from grant-based support to market-driven funding, the government has established the Rs. 1 lakh crore Urban Challenge Fund, which could raise Rs. 4 lakh crore over the course of five years using blended approaches. At least 50% of project funding must be raised by urban local authorities through PPPs, bank loans, or municipal bonds, with a 25% contribution from the Center. With the use of new guarantee programs, this strategy seeks to enhance creditworthiness, financial discipline, and transparency, particularly in smaller cities.

ola-electric-investment-ev-battery-local:
Summary:

To improve localisation and vertical integration, Ola Electric Mobility has authorised an investment of Rs. 2,000 crore (US$214 million) in its companies that produce EVs and battery cells. By May 2027, the money will be transferred to Ola Electric Technologies and Ola Cell Technologies. This action is intended to increase cost competitiveness in India's expanding EV market, decrease reliance on outside suppliers, and improve manufacturing efficiency. After the investment, Ola Electric will continue to own all of the subsidiaries. Ola is concentrating on battery technology and domestic cell production in the face of growing competition from Ather Energy, Bajaj Auto, and TVS Motor. The company is growing its lithium-ion cell facility in Tamil Nadu and has already invested Rs. 5,300 crore in production and research and development.

Summary:

Tencent's announcement of an investment of more than Rs. 10 crore (US$1.08 million) in India's Animation, Visual Effects, Gaming, and Comics (AVGC) industry reflects growing international trust in the nation's expanding creative economy. To bolster the gaming and animation ecosystem, the company has established multi-year relationships with the Game Developers Association of India (GDAI) and the Services Export Promotion Council (SEPC). In order to empower creators and developers, the initiative will concentrate on skill development, training programs, mentorship, and industry engagement. It supports India's efforts to grow its "Orange Economy," which is fuelled by innovation and digital content. By 2030, the AVGC industry would need close to two million workers. Tencent's action is intended to improve India's long-term ecosystem growth, innovation, and access to international markets.

india-exports-growth-april-2026-trade-de:
Summary:

India's overall exports, comprising goods and services, increased by 13.59% from April 2025 to Rs. 7.49 lakh crore (US$80.80 billion) in April 2026. Services exports grew to Rs. 3.45 lakh crore, while merchandise exports jumped 13.78% to Rs. 4.03 lakh crore. Although imports totalled Rs. 8.21 lakh crore, the trade deficit shrank to US$7.81 billion from US$11.16 billion in the previous year. Additionally, non-petroleum exports increased by 9.01%. Petroleum products, electronics, engineering items, meat, dairy and poultry products, and pharmaceuticals were the main industries propelling export development. The first month of 2026 saw a solid performance in external commerce, with petroleum exports rising 34.66%, electronic goods rising 40.31%, and engineering goods rising 8.76%.

Source: IBEF

india-auto-sales-april-2026-growth-passe:
Summary:

According to SIAM, domestic dispatches from manufacturers to dealers increased 25.4% year over year to 4,37,312 units in April 2026, marking a solid start for India's passenger vehicle industry in FY27. Strong demand for passenger cars and utility vehicles fuelled growth, maintaining the momentum from the second half of FY26. Passenger car sales rose 32.7% to 1,20,945 units, while utility vehicle dispatches rose 21.5% to 2,44,280 units. In the automotive industry, two-wheeler sales increased by 28.4% in April, while three-wheeler deliveries increased by 32.8%. Vehicle wholesales as a whole rose 27.9% to 23,16,671 units. Growing consumer confidence, rising earnings, and consistent demand throughout India's automotive market were cited by industry analysts as the reasons for the expansion. Overall, demand remained strong despite geopolitical concerns and commodities prices.

Summary:

According to a Kofluence analysis, the influencer marketing sector in India is expected to grow to Rs. 5,000 crore by 2027 as the creative economy gets more institutionalised and structured. The sector is becoming more formalised due to stricter rules, increased creator professionalisation, broader adoption of AI tools, and stronger brand accountability. As influencer-led marketing ecosystems mature, the industry is moving from unofficial brand ties to quantifiable and carefully managed alliances. Millions of digital artists are currently supported by India's creator economy, and Tier-II and Tier-III cities are increasingly participating. Growth is also being supported by rising internet access and consumption of digital media. In the advertising, media, and technology sectors across the country, AI-powered analytics and campaign optimisation tools are also increasing productivity and assisting firms in more accurately assessing campaign results.

Summary:

With a strategy centred on exports, value addition, and sustainable fisheries development, India is bolstering its position in the global seafood market. Union Ministers Piyush Goyal and Rajiv Ranjan Singh discussed ways to increase the competitiveness of seafood exports. To improve the seafood ecosystem, the government will host a two-day "Chintan Shivir" in Visakhapatnam on June 5 - 6, 2026, involving ministries, exporters, entrepreneurs, and fisheries departments. In order to facilitate the adoption of technology, infrastructure, and value-added production, authorities are investigating a specific PLI framework for seafood MSMEs. The project intends to grow the number of seafood exporters from 1,200 to around 5,000. india-ccdb-chair-cybersecurity-it-standa:
Summary:

The Common Criteria Development Board (CCDB), a crucial international organization in charge of worldwide IT security evaluation standards, has been chaired by India. This breakthrough represents a significant turning point in India's increasing leadership in digital governance, cybersecurity, and reliable technological systems. The Common Criteria Recognition Arrangement (CCRA), which permits participating nations to mutually recognise IT security certifications, is managed by the CCDB. India's nomination is a sign of growing international trust in its institutional capacity and cybersecurity knowledge. It is anticipated that the chairmanship will increase India's influence in developing secure digital infrastructure standards and cybersecurity frameworks. By boosting international recognition of India's certification ecosystem and fostering global collaboration on cyber resilience and trusted technology systems, it might also help Indian IT and cybersecurity companies.

Artificial intelligence integration helps process large volumes of operational data, automate routine tasks, and improve service outcomes across the network. Summary:

With the introduction of two AI-enabled forecasting products by the Ministry of Earth Sciences (MoES), India has made significant progress in bolstering its weather forecasting ecosystem. Union Minister Dr. Jitendra Singh unveiled the AI-based "Forecast of Monsoon Advance over Different Parts of the Country" and a high-resolution rainfall forecast pilot for Uttar Pradesh. The systems provide hyper-local and impact-based weather services. The Uttar Pradesh model uses AI and multisource data to provide weekly forecasts up to four weeks in advance for 16 states and over 3,000 sub-districts. The AI-based "Forecast of Monsoon Advance over Different Parts of the Country" and a high-resolution rainfall forecast pilot for Uttar Pradesh were presented by Union Minister Dr. Jitendra Singh. The systems, which were created by IMD, IITM Pune, and NCMRWF, offer impactbased and hyper-local weather services. To assist farmers and policymakers, the monsoon platform provides weekly projections for 16 states and more than 3,000 sub-districts up to four weeks in advance. The Uttar Pradesh model uses multi-source data and artificial intelligence to provide 1-km rainfall projections. Forecast accuracy has improved by about 40% in India, enhancing decisionmaking across sectors and climate resilience.

india-psb-record-profit-fy26-credit-grow:
Summary:

In 2025–2026, Public Sector Banks (PSBs) in India recorded their highest-ever net profit of Rs. 1.98 lakh crore (US$ 22.59 billion), marking the fourth consecutive year of profitability. According to the Ministry of Finance, this performance was fueled by strong business growth, improved asset quality, and increased operational efficiency. Aggregate PSB business increased 12.8% year-overyear to Rs. 156.3 lakh crore (US$ 1.78 trillion), while deposits increased 15.7% to Rs. 127 lakh crore (US$ 1.45 trillion). Strong company development, better asset quality, and increased operational efficiency were the main drivers of this performance, according to the Ministry of Finance. While deposits jumped 10.6% to Rs. 156.3 lakh crore (US$1.78 trillion), aggregate PSB business expanded 12.8% year over year to Rs. 283.3 lakh crore (US$3.23 trillion). Strong credit demand, particularly in the retail, agricultural, and MSME sectors, drove a 15.7% increase in gross advances to Rs. 127 lakh crore (US$1.45 trillion). With GNPA at 1.93% and Net NPA at 0.39%, stress assets saw a significant decline. Profitability and capital strength both greatly increased.

lanxess-hpcl-lubricant-partnership-india:
Summary:

In response to the growing demand for high-performance lubricants in India's expanding industrial ecosystem, German specialty chemicals company LANXESS and Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Limited (HPCL) have signed a memorandum of understanding (MoU) aiming to improve availability of advanced lubricant solutions in key aviation and industrial segments. The partnership will explore short-, medium-, and long-term business development opportunities while expanding access to premium LANXESS-branded products. Leaders from both companies emphasized that combining HPCL's strong domestic presence with LANXESS' technical expertise will drive growth, improve supply chains, and support industrial modernization and efficiency. The goal of the memorandum of understanding (MoU) is to increase the availability of cutting-edge lubrication solutions in important industrial and aviation sectors. The partnership will increase access to high- end LANXESS-branded goods while investigating short-, medium-, and long-term commercial development potential. It is a reflection of India's expanding industrial ecosystem's growing need for high-performance lubricants. According to LANXESS, the partnership will improve accessibility and standards in the industrial and aviation sectors. Leaders from both businesses emphasized that integrating LANXESS' technical know-how with HPCL's robust local presence will boost supply chains, promote industrial modernization and efficiency, and spur growth.

Source: IBEF

hindustan-zinc-group-nirmal-zinc-wire-pl:
Summary:

In order to strengthen India's downstream zinc manufacturing ecosystem and promote value-added applications, Hindustan Zinc Limited, a Vedanta Group company and the largest integrated zinc producer in the world, has signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with Group Nirmal to establish a zinc wire manufacturing facility at its Zinc Industrial Park in Bhilwara, Rajasthan. The project, developed with RIICO, will boost industrial growth, attract investment, strengthen the durability of bridges, railways, pipelines, and ports. The factory will make zinc wire for the automotive, industrial engineering, renewable energy, and infrastructure sectors using Special High Grade (SHG) zinc from Hindustan Zinc. The program seeks to advance value-added applications and fortify India's downstream zinc production ecosystem. Bridges, railroads, pipelines, and ports are made more durable by the use of zinc wire in thermal spray coating, which shields steel structures against corrosion. The project, which was created in collaboration with RIICO, would enhance supply chains, increase industrial growth, draw in investment, and create jobs in Rajasthan's manufacturing sector.

Source: IBEF

india-mlff-tolling-cost-efficiency-highw:
Summary:

Union Minister for Road Transport and Highways Nitin Gadkari said the proposed Multi Lane Free Flow (MLFF) toll system will cut toll collection operating costs from 12–15% to about 3–4%, improving highway efficiency in India. The system will use ANPR cameras, FASTag integration and GPS-based tracking to enable barrier-free tolling without stopping at plazas. It is expected to reduce congestion fuel consumption and travel time on national highways. The government is running pilot projects and technology integration steps for phased rollout across major corridors. Gadkari said digital tolling will improve transparency user convenience and operational efficiency. Aligned with Bharatmala Pariyojana and PM Gati Shakti, MLFF supports modern transport infrastructure and smart mobility, potentially lowering logistics costs.

Source: IBEF

india-ai-semiconductors-railways-growth:
Summary:

Speaking at the CII Annual Business Summit 2026, Union Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw stated that AI, semiconductors, and railroads will propel India's next technological and economic expansion. He emphasized the quick modernization of railroads, the production of electronics, and the advancement of semiconductors, all of which were aided by investment and reforms. In ten years, India electrified 49,000 km and installed 36,000 km of tracks, with railway capital expenditures reaching approximately Rs. 2.72 lakh crore. He emphasized how AI may revolutionize various industries by increasing productivity, quality, and efficiency. Through the India Semiconductor Mission and IndiaAI Mission, the government is advancing digital infrastructure, AI, and semiconductor manufacturing. According to experts, these advancements will promote the idea of a technology-driven Viksit Bharat by bolstering India's manufacturing base, generating jobs, and enhancing global competitiveness.

Source: IBEF

made-in-india-brand-scheme-boosts-local-:
Summary:

India is getting ready to introduce a "Made in India Brand Scheme" to boost homegrown production and lessen reliance on imports in several important industries. At the CII Annual Business Summit 2026, DPIIT Secretary Amardeep Singh Bhatia unveiled the program, which seeks to identify approximately 100 items that are either not produced in India or are produced in insufficient amounts. To promote both domestic and international production, the government has shortlisted items such as motorcycle parts and car axles. With a dedicated logo and certification system that guarantees standards and value addition, the program will establish a quality-backed identity. Positive outcomes from a steel industry pilot led to plans for a wider rollout following stakeholder engagements. The government encourages FTAs, robust supply chains, and global hubs for AI investments.

Source: IBEF

india-gdp-growth-forecast-sbi-fy27-66-pe:
Summary:

An SBI Research research published in May 2026 projects that India's economy would expand by 6.6% in FY27. According to the research, India has demonstrated resilience in the face of geopolitical tensions and worldwide uncertainties. While Q4 FY26 growth is anticipated to be close to 7.2%, GDP growth for 2025–2026 is projected to be 7.5%. Economic momentum is anticipated to be supported by robust domestic consumption, growing rural demand, and consistent credit expansion. Despite a minor slowdown in the fourth quarter, high-frequency indicators point to strong activity. In FY26, bank credit growth increased from 11% to 16.1%, with incremental credit totaling Rs. 29.5 lakh crore. Demand in both rural and urban areas remained robust, despite ongoing threats of inflation due to oil prices and El Niño circumstances.

Source: IBEF

jk-limestone-auctions-boost-cement-indus:
Summary:

The second round of limestone block auctions in Jammu and Kashmir will be held by the Ministry of Mines in an effort to boost industrial growth and increase the supply of raw materials for the infrastructure and cement industries. In addition to creating jobs and stimulating the local economy, the program is anticipated to increase investment in mining, cement production, and related businesses. It is a component of the government's plan to boost private sector involvement, expedite mineral exploration, and enhance resource utilization. Large limestone reserves in Jammu and Kashmir offer great potential for the mineral-based and cement sectors. It is anticipated that the auctions will boost downstream industry growth, improve mining infrastructure, and increase investor engagement. Additionally, the action supports transparent auction processes for sustainable development, ease of doing business, and mining reforms.

Source: IBEF

novartis-expands-india-role-global-drug-:
Summary:

By increasing research, development, and clinical activities throughout the nation, Novartis is bolstering India's position in its worldwide drug development strategy. Participation in international drug development initiatives is made possible by India's emergence as a major center for scientific talent, digital skills, and advanced research support. In order to support clinical research, data analytics, AI-enabled research, and regulatory operations throughout the drug development lifecycle, the company hires scientists, data specialists, and IT professionals in India. Novartis emphasized that pharmaceutical innovation is being accelerated and global healthcare outcomes are being improved by India's robust talent ecosystem and expanding digital infrastructure. With the help of highly qualified personnel, cost competitiveness, and biotechnology know-how, India is also becoming a hub for biomedical research, AI-driven drug discovery, and digital healthcare technologies, bolstering its position in the global pharmaceutical industry.

india-women-maritime-sector-growth:
Summary:

Women's engagement in India's maritime sector has increased by 340% since 2020, according to Union Minister for Ports, Shipping, and Waterways Sarbananda Sonowal, underscoring the government's commitment to an inclusive and future-ready workforce. He stated that programs like "Sagar Mein Samman" are promoting dignity, inclusiveness, and leadership chances for women in maritime professions while speaking at the 10th Indian Ocean Dialogue in New Delhi. He stated that the rise is in line with India's larger marine vision, which is focused on social empowerment, labor diversity, and economic prosperity. Sonowal further emphasized that, under frameworks like SAGAR and MAHASAGAR, the "human element" continues to be crucial to India's marine strategy, fostering regional cooperation, sustainability, security, and fair progress for everyone in the Indian Ocean Region.

Source: IBEF

india-uae-defense-partnership-cooperatio:
Summary:

It is anticipated that Prime Minister Narendra Modi's next trip to the UAE will increase cooperation in security, defense, and strategic areas and fortify the India-UAE Strategic Defense Partnership. The visit comes after UAE President Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan visited India in January 2026 and signed a Letter of Intent on Strategic Defense Partnership. The framework seeks to improve cooperation in innovative technologies, cyber security, counterterrorism, training, interoperability, and defense production. In addition to aiming for bilateral commerce of US$200 billion by 2032 and boosting regional security and economic involvement throughout the Gulf area, India and the UAE are increasing their cooperation in trade, energy, infrastructure, artificial intelligence, space technology, and connectivity.

Source: IBEF

india-construction-equipment-market-grow:
Summary:

India's construction equipment market grew significantly in 2025–2026 due to the country's expanding infrastructure and growing demand for Indian-made machinery worldwide. The Ministry of Heavy Industries reports that overall sales were 1,40,191 units, up 3% from the year before. Exports increased 31.5% year over year to 16,885 units, demonstrating India's increasing competitiveness in the production of construction equipment. Large investments in mining, highways, railroads, urban expansion, and energy projects, as well as a rise in construction industry mechanization, all contributed to growth. Localization, capacity expansion, and technology adoption were enhanced by government programs like the PM Gati Shakti program, the National Infrastructure Pipeline (NIP), and the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme. India's standing in the global supply chain was further reinforced by growing exports to Africa, Southeast Asia, the Middle East, and Latin America.

Source: IBEF

india-private-capex-growth-fy26:
Summary:

The Confederation of Indian Industry (CII) reports that private sector capital expenditure announcements in India increased 67% year over year to Rs. 7.70 lakh crore (US$81.48 billion) during FY26, indicating increased corporate confidence and growth driven by infrastructure. Data centers, electronics, semiconductors, autos, chemicals, steel, and renewable energy were the main areas of investment. Companies were encouraged to invest in technology and expand their capacity by policy backing, production-linked incentives, growing infrastructure, and rising consumption. According to CII, investments are also being drawn to developing industries like green hydrogen, battery production, artificial intelligence, electric cars, and digital infrastructure. Stronger financial sheets, increased business profitability, and improved credit availability all contributed to India's growing private investment activity.

Source: IBEF

mediatek-noida-rd-center-semiconductor-i:
Summary:

By establishing a new research and development (R&D) facility in Noida, MediaTek has increased its footprint in India and strengthened the country's position as a developing hub for semiconductor and technology innovation. The 1.04 lakh square foot facility, which is situated in Sector 94 of BPTP Capital City, will help the company expand its technical staff and conduct cutting-edge chip design research. To speed up semiconductor innovation, the center will collaborate with MediaTek's Bengaluru operations. Over a ten-year period, the business has rented the Noida facility for around Rs. 144 crore. According to industry experts, Noida is becoming a significant center for technology because of its enhanced infrastructure, rising demand for Grade-A office space, close proximity to the future Jewar International Airport, and more foreign investment.

Source: IBEF

adobe-noida-office-expansion-india-tech-:
Summary:

By renting almost 1.58 lakh square feet of office space at Max Square in Noida, Adobe has increased its footprint in India. Adobe's engineering and customer-focused operations in India would be strengthened by the facility, which is situated on the Noida-Greater Noida Expressway and will house more than 700 workers. The growth is a reflection of Noida's increasing significance as a top location for multinational corporations, technology enterprises, and global capability centers looking for better connection and high-end office facilities. According to industry insiders, the region's commercial real estate activity is being boosted by projects like the forthcoming Jewar International Airport. Adobe's success also demonstrates India's emerging position as a worldwide center for technology and innovation, bolstered by the country's expanding digital infrastructure and highly qualified workforce.

pmegp-msme-employment-growth-india:
Summary:

As of March 31, 2026, the MSME Ministry reports that the Prime Minister's Employment Generation Programme (PMEGP) had created jobs for over 36.33 lakh individuals and helped over four lakh micro-enterprises throughout India. It has encouraged self-employment and entrepreneurship since 2008 - 09, particularly in rural and semi-urban areas. The program, which is run by KVIC in collaboration with State KVIBs, KVIC Directorates, and DICs, provides margin money subsidies to establish microbusinesses in manufacturing and services, promoting inclusive growth and local economic activity. Project costs have increased to Rs. 25 lakh for services and Rs. 1 crore for manufacturing due to reforms. It promotes rural industrialization and MSME growth in industries like food processing by supporting women, aspirational areas, and first-generation businesses.

Source: IBEF

india-eu-fta-trade-investment-growth:
Summary:

The Free Trade Agreement (FTA) between the EU and India is one of the biggest in the world, affecting two billion people and accounting for one-third of world trade. One-fourth of the world's GDP and Rs. 1,029.93 lakh crore (US$11 trillion) in trade flows will come from what Darpan Jain called the "mother of all deals." Goods, services, digital trade, telecom, finance, intellectual property, and regulation are all covered by Beyond Tariff Cuts. likely to increase supply chain integration, investment, and manufacturing cooperation. Automobiles, textiles, chemicals, leather, and digital services are important industries. At Rs. 39.42 lakh crore, services exports are growing more quickly than goods exports, which are at Rs. 41.38 lakh crore. Improve long-term investment, predictability, and market access between the EU and India.

india-france-space-technology-collaborat:
Summary:

Dr. Jitendra Singh and French Minister Prof. Philippe Baptiste met virtually, strengthening India and France's strategic collaboration in research, technology, and space exploration. Both sides talked about increasing collaboration in advanced materials, ocean sciences, applied mathematics, artificial intelligence, and space technology. Collaboration in science and space was emphasized as one of the main foundations of India-France relations, fostering technological advancement and closer interpersonal links. Cooperation in innovation-driven industries is anticipated to increase during the 2026 Indo-French Year of Innovation. With a review of ISRO-CNES cooperation in satellite missions, Earth observation, launch systems, and maritime awareness, space continued to be a top priority. Additionally, France expressed interest in deep ocean projects, human spaceflight, astronaut training, microgravity research, and support for India's Gaganyaan mission.

india-d2c-logistics-courier-aggregators-:
Summary:

Courier aggregators are driving the rapid growth of India's direct-to-consumer (D2C) ecosystem by converting logistics into a technologically advanced system for online firms. Small retailers and new direct-to-consumer businesses can access enterprise-level logistics through these platforms without having to make significant investments in warehousing or shipping infrastructure. They optimize delivery, cut expenses, and improve customer satisfaction by combining several courier partners into a single interface. Logistics companies now link companies with more than 40 courier partners, delivering to more than 220 countries and covering more than 19,000 PIN codes throughout India. Through analytics, automated order allocation, tracking, and returns management, courier aggregators are also becoming into strategic growth enablers that increase productivity and profitability. India's digital commerce environment is being further strengthened by AI-driven solutions.

Source: IBEF

india-startup-incubators-innovation-grow:
Summary:

According to NITI Aayog, incubators are essential to bolstering India's startup ecosystem since they offer market connections, infrastructure, finance access, and mentorship. Atal Innovation Mission (AIM) Mission Director Deepak Bagla emphasized incubators as essential foundations for growing creative firms during a regional innovation event in Mohali. In order to foster cooperation and create a robust regional innovation network, the event brought together government, business, academics, incubators, and startup players. Enhancing collaboration among northern states and Union Territories, including as Punjab, Haryana, Himachal Pradesh, Jammu & Kashmir, Ladakh, Uttarakhand, and Delhi, was its main goal. The establishment of the Punjab & J&K Incubation Consortium (PI-RAHI), which aims to strengthen regional innovation relationships and encourage youth entrepreneurship and startup growth, was a significant highlight.

samsung-ai-business-experience-studio-in:
Summary:

By establishing an AI-powered Business Experience Studio (BES) in Gurugram to highlight digital transformation solutions for companies, Samsung Electronics has increased its corporate presence in India. The studio showcases linked technology and AI-enabled ecosystems for industries like banking, retail, healthcare, hospitality, and education. It enables businesses to see how Samsung products, such as smart displays, mobility devices, AI platforms, and Knox security systems, are used in the real world. The project is in line with India's expanding use of AI and digital transformation in many sectors of the economy. According to Samsung, the BES will serve as a cocreation hub where companies and technology partners can create specialized solutions for security, efficiency, and customer engagement. The company intends to expand further throughout India and currently runs a comparable studio in Mumbai.

india-exports-growth-fy26-services-trade:
Summary:

Despite world economic uncertainty, India's total exports of goods and services increased 4.6% year over year to a record ₹75.43 lakh crore (US$863.11 billion) in FY26 from ₹71.24 lakh crore in FY25. Data from the Commerce Ministry indicates that this shows how resilient overseas trade is in the face of declining global demand and geopolitical upheavals. While product exports shown consistent pace across sectors, services exports fueled growth through robust IT, business, financial, and professional services. The achievement demonstrates India's growing competitiveness in foreign markets and inclusion into global value chains. Alongside the growing need for pharmaceuticals, engineering, electronics, and digital services, government attempts to improve manufacturing, logistics, and market diversification boosted growth.

india-vietnam-trade-partnership-2030:
Summary:

Following discussions between Prime Minister Narendra Modi and President To Lam in New Delhi, India and Vietnam decided to strengthen their partnership and established a $25 billion trade objective by 2030. Defense and security, commerce and investment, atomic and renewable energy, research and technology, marine cooperation, space, and people-to-people exchanges were all discussed. For more economic participation, both nations upgraded their relationship to an Enhanced Comprehensive Strategic Partnership. Thirteen Memorandums of Understanding pertaining to rare earth minerals, digital payments, healthcare, tourism, education, culture, and academic collaboration were inked. With bilateral trade already at $16 billion, India and Vietnam have committed to strengthen resilience supplies, promote My Son Temple Center, link UPI with Vietnam payment systems, and increase agricultural trade.

india-defense-manufacturing-exports-grow:
Summary:

Over the past ten years, India's defense industry has seen tremendous change thanks to domestic innovation, the adoption of new technologies, and an increase in the involvement of the private sector. Union Minister Dr. Jitendra Singh stated at the North Tech Symposium 2026 in Prayagraj that defense manufacturing had increased by 174% in ten years to ₹1.54 lakh crore (US$16.45 billion). India's transition from a significant importer to an emerging worldwide exporter of defense equipment was highlighted by the 34-fold increase in defense exports to ₹23,622 crore (US$2.52 billion). About ₹15,000 crore of exports come from the private sector. Domestic manufacturing has grown thanks to government assistance and legislative changes. The ₹6.81 lakh crore defense budget for 2026–2027 would improve national security readiness and cutting-edge technologies.

india-services-pmi-april-2026-growth:
Summary:

In April 2026, India's services sector grew at its quickest rate in five months due to robust logistics activity, growing new business inflows, and strong domestic demand. The Business Activity Index increased to 58.8 in April from 57.5 in March, the highest growth since November 2025, according to the HSBC India Services PMI. Expansion is indicated by a PMI greater than 50. Competitive pricing, robust customer demand, and increased need for logistics and relocation services all contributed to growth. As a result of strong consumption and better domestic conditions, new orders also rose. However, supply-chain issues and geopolitical tensions caused export demand to decline. Businesses hired more workers, but input costs remained high because of labor, fuel, and transportation constraints.

Source: IBEF

india-steel-industry-growth-april-2026:
Summary:

In April 2026, India's steel industry continued to grow rapidly due to strong demand from the manufacturing, building, and infrastructure sectors. The Ministry of Steel reports that while finished steel output increased 3.4% to 13.05 million tonnes, crude steel production increased 5.8% year over year to 14.09 million tonnes. Consumption of finished steel increased by 8.1% to 12.99 million tonnes, indicating continued industrial activity. India was a marginal net importer with imports of 0.68 million tonnes and exports of 0.47 million tonnes. Both imports and exports increased significantly from April 2025. In 2025–2026, capacity was close to 220 MTPA; by 2030, it is expected to reach 300 MTPA. Prices are rising, major companies are increasing capacity, and green steel efforts are spreading.

Source: IBEF

india-ev-sales-growth-april-2026:
Summary:

According to FADA data, passenger car sales in India's electric vehicle (EV) industry increased 75.14% year over year to 23,506 units in April 2026. With 8,543 units, up 77.17%, Tata Motors was in the lead, followed by JSW MG Motor with 5,006 units and Mahindra & Mahindra with 5,413. Increased customer confidence in EVs, expanded model availability, and enhanced charging infrastructure all contributed to growth. Sales of two-wheelers increased by 60.73% to 1,48,740 units. Ather sold 27,034 units, up 102.78%, Bajaj 32,898 units, and TVS Motor 37,683 units, up 88.64%. Sales of three-wheeler EVs increased 3.3% year over year to 64,549 units. Overall development demonstrates India's rapid transition to sustainable transportation, which is backed by investment, technology, and policy in the country's largest metropolitan areas.

Source: IBEF

Growing consumption and value-added products contributed to the popularity of instant coffee. Stable supply chains, increased crop productivity, and favorable prices all helped to boost exports. india-economic-resilience-moodys-report:
Summary:

According to Moody's Ratings, India has excellent macroeconomic fundamentals and stable policy, making it one of the most resilient emerging markets to global shocks. Resilience in the face of global uncertainties is supported by strong domestic demand, a diversified economy, and a stable financial system. India's capacity to resist outside pressures is strengthened by steady growth supported by reforms and responsible budgetary management. Reduced reliance on exports also lessens the impact of changes in global demand. The report highlights a strong banking sector, improved asset quality and effective regulation. Growth and investor confidence are increased when the government prioritizes manufacturing, infrastructure, and digitization. India is predicted to continue to be a major global economic engine due to its favorable demographics, growing consumption, and ongoing reforms.

india-mlff-tolling-nh48-stop-free-system:
Summary:

On the Surat-Bharuch section of NH-48 in Gujarat, the National Highways Authority of India implemented the country's first stop-free tolling system utilizing Multi-Lane Free Flow (MLFF) technology. It eliminates the need for cars to stop at the Chorayasi toll plaza, allowing for smooth movement. Tolls are automatically deducted while cars travel at regular speeds thanks to Automatic Number Plate Recognition and FASTag. The system ensures smooth traffic flow while lowering emissions, fuel consumption, travel time, and congestion. About 41,500 cars used it on its first day. MLFF is anticipated to grow nationally and save fuel expenses by about Rs. 1,500 crore per year as part of a larger modernization drive.

Source: IBEF

Projects like the four-laning of Taloda-Burhanpur and the six-laning of Nagpur-Bhandara and Pune-Satara were discussed at the meeting in New Delhi. Despite pricing pressure, regulatory obstacles, and geopolitical unpredictability, India's pharmaceutical exports shown remarkable resiliency in FY26, surpassing Rs. 2.86 lakh crore (US$31 billion). The expansion demonstrates India's standing as a top worldwide provider of reasonably priced medications, bolstered by a strong manufacturing base and a wide range of export markets. Generics, bulk medications, vaccines, and biosimilars are important markets with demand from the US, Europe, Africa, and developing nations. India's place in global supply chains was strengthened by strong worldwide demand. Nonetheless, the growth rate was very slow, indicating a cautious attitude among businesses. upi-growth-india-digital-payments-econom:
Summary:

Since its inception in 2016, the Unified Payments Interface (UPI) has grown to become the largest real-time payment platform in the world, accounting for almost half of all instantaneous transactions worldwide. It developed into the foundation of India's digital economy, encouraging smooth payments and financial inclusion across the country. It increased dramatically from 373 transactions at first to over 24,162 crore transactions in 2025–2026. The value of the transaction increased from Rs. 0.07 lakh crore to Rs. 314 lakh crore. The average daily activity is 66 crore transactions. With the help of hundreds of banks, UPI currently accounts for around 85% of digital payments in India. It powers both high-value transfers and small retail payments while growing internationally, bolstering India's fintech presence, and continuing to innovate in credit, cross-border services, and the sustainable development of digital public infrastructure.

It is anticipated that the six-lane expressway, which can be expanded to eight lanes, will cut travel times from ten to twelve hours to roughly six to seven hours. Nearly one-third of the record peak electricity demand of 256 GW was satisfied by renewable energy, marking a significant milestone for India's power sector. This illustrates the quick expansion of solar, wind, and hydropower capacity as well as their growing contribution to the country's grid. The accomplishment demonstrates advancements in the direction of a cleaner energy mix and successful policies that encourage the growth of renewable energy sources and grid integration. Additionally, it lessens reliance on fossil fuels while satisfying the growing demand brought on by urbanization and growth. Improved forecasting, storage systems, and grid management enabled renewable energy to meet peak demand. This achievement was aided by investments in wind farms, solar parks, and green corridors. In the upcoming years, India is anticipated to steadily increase its capacity for sustainable energy.

india-female-workforce-participation-gro:
Summary:

Over the previous seven years, India's female labor force participation rate has increased dramatically from 23.3% to 40%. The growth is a result of increased employment options, skill development, and encouragement for entrepreneurship, all of which contribute to a greater inclusion of women in the workforce. Self-employment, better access to education, and government empowerment initiatives have all contributed to an increase in participation in both rural and urban areas. Women have found employment in manufacturing, services, agriculture, and the gig economy thanks to initiatives in financial inclusion, social security, digital access, and vocational training. This advancement was also aided by changes to working conditions, maternity benefits, and workplace safety. Increased female participation is anticipated to help India's national progress toward gender equality by increasing productivity, economic growth, and inclusive development.

india-iip-growth-march-2026-manufacturin:
Summary:

In March 2026, India's Index of Industrial Production (IIP) increased 4.1% year over year, demonstrating the country's ongoing industrial activity and manufacturing-led growth. The IIP increased from 166.3 in March 2025 to 173.2 thanks to increases in manufacturing of 4.3%, mining of 5.5%, and electricity of 0.8%. The expansion was aided by increased domestic demand, infrastructure spending, and improved capacity utilization. 14 out of 23 industry groups in manufacturing saw growth, with basic metals (8.6%), automobiles and trailers (18.1%), and machinery (11.2%) leading the way. While infrastructure and construction products gained 6.7%, capital goods increased 14.6%, indicating robust investment activity. The 5.3% growth in consumer durables is indicative of strong demand and general manufacturing competitiveness.

Source: IBEF

India reached a significant milestone in international commerce in 2025–2026 when its total exports of goods and services exceeded Rs. 81.28 lakh crore (US$860 billion). The accomplishment demonstrates resilience in the face of global difficulties and improved integration with global value chains. Engineering items, electronics, medicines, chemicals, gems and jewelry, and agricultural products were among the major contributors, demonstrating widespread export development across industries. Through free trade agreements with industrialized economies, the government hopes to increase commerce, improve market access, and create jobs. The trade ecosystem is being strengthened by ongoing reforms, industry collaboration, and policy support. This achievement helps India reach its goal of exporting Rs. 189.08 lakh crore (US$2 trillion) by 2030 as part of the Viksit Bharat vision.

india-engineering-exports-new-zealand-ft:
Summary:

Following the India-New Zealand Free Trade Agreement, it is anticipated that India's engineering exports to New Zealand will quadruple in five years. Exports could increase to Rs. 1,890.8-2,836.2 crore (US$280-300 million) from Rs. 1,328.39 crore (US$140.5 million). The FTA lowers trade obstacles and increases competitiveness by granting Indian goods zero-duty access. According to EEPC India, the deal will provide steady market access, which will greatly help the engineering sector, particularly MSMEs. In 2025–2026, exports to New Zealand increased by 8% over the previous year. Cars, dairy equipment, scientific and medical equipment, and metal products are among the main exports. The agreement backs India's plan to use free trade agreements (FTAs) to increase international trade and diversify export markets.

Summary:

With strong policy support, India is becoming a reliable partner in the global electronics production and supply chain, according to Union Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw. He discussed a Rs. 1.41 lakh crore (US$15 billion) project with a 1 GW hyperscale AI data center, constructed with Adani ConneX and Airtel Nxtra, during his speech at the Google Cloud India AI Hub opening in Visakhapatnam. For the project, Andhra Pradesh has set aside 600 acres. Mobile phones are among India's biggest exports, and half of the country's electronics needs are satisfied domestically. India is already a leader in IT services and growing electronics manufacturing. By transforming Visakhapatnam into a "AI Patnam," the project hopes to promote innovation and digital change across the country.

india-dpi-2-productivity-growth-2047:
Summary:

Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) 2.0 is positioned as a key factor in long-term growth and resilience in India's DPI@2047 program. It was introduced by NITI Aayog and moves the emphasis from welfare delivery via Aadhaar and UPI to a productivity-led strategy that enhances market access, efficiency, and livelihoods. MSMEs, agriculture, healthcare, education, credit, energy, and social protection are the eight priority sectors. The plan emphasizes integrated digital platforms, artificial intelligence, and district-level execution. DPI 2.0 can increase productivity and lower risks from global shocks like energy price volatility, according to Chief Economic Advisor V. Anantha Nageswaran. In order to help India achieve its goal of having a US$30 trillion economy by 2047, pilot projects will start in 2026–2027.

Developed in collaboration with Google, AdaniConneX, and Airtel Nxtra, the project is one of India's biggest FDI projects, requiring an investment of Rs. 1.41 lakh crore (US$15 billion). Nearly 600 acres in Tharluwada, Adavivaram, and Rambilli will make up the 1 gigawatt AI data center. india-inland-waterways-growth-sustainabl:
Summary:

Using rivers, canals, and backwaters, India is quickly developing inland waterways into a contemporary transportation network. It is one of the biggest networks in the world, with 111 national waterways spanning 20,187 km throughout 23 states and four UTs. There are already 32 functioning waterways spanning 5,100 km as of 2026; in five years, that number is expected to rise to 52, and 20 more are being suggested. Because one vessel may replace 125 trucks, water transportation is more affordable, environmentally friendly, and uses up to six times less energy than road or rail. Both passenger and cargo traffic are growing rapidly. By 2030 and 2047, future goals call for increased shares and volumes, which will be bolstered by significant government initiatives and programs that promote the expansion of sustainable logistics.

india-new-zealand-fta-trade-investment-g:
Summary:

A significant step toward deepening economic connections was taken on April 27, 2026, when India and New Zealand inked a historic Free Trade Agreement (FTA). The agreement, which was signed by Piyush Goyal and Todd McClay, benefits industries including textiles, pharmaceuticals, and engineering items by allowing duty-free access to 100% of Indian exports to New Zealand. Within five years, it hopes to quadruple bilateral trade to ₹46,220 crore (US$5 billion) and enhance cooperation, mobility, and services trade. Additionally, during the course of 15 years, New Zealand has invested ₹18.49 lakh crore (US$20 billion) in India. In addition to encouraging investment, technology transfer, and greater Indo-Pacific trade integration overall, the pact lowers tariffs, facilitates commerce, supports visas, and safeguards delicate industries like dairy.

With the start of its first commercial seaplane flights following DGCA certification to SkyHop Aviation, India is embarking on a new phase of regional air connectivity. An important development in India's aviation industry is the Air Operator Certificate, which permits operations to start. Seaplanes improve access in areas where airport construction is challenging since they can take off and land on water. This helps the government achieve its objective of improving multimodal and last-mile connectivity. With Lakshadweep as a crucial route, SkyHop intends to connect island and coastal areas with 19-seater aircraft. It is anticipated that the service will shorten travel times, facilitate access to isolated tourist areas, and strengthen local economies. Operational viability under general Indian conditions is further confirmed by successful trials in Uttarakhand.
